1479	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentExecuteLoopCommand() *cobra.Command {
  1480		cmd := &cobra.Command{
  1481			Use:   "execute-loop",
  1482			Short: "Drain the single-project execution-ready bead queue",
  1483			Long: `execute-loop is the primary queue-driven execution surface. It scans the
  1484	target project's execution-ready bead queue, claims the next ready bead,
  1485	runs "ddx agent execute-bead" on it from the project root, records the
  1486	structured result, and continues until no unattempted ready work remains.
  1487	
  1488	Reach for execute-loop by default. Use "ddx agent execute-bead" directly
  1489	only as the primitive for debugging or re-running one specific bead.
  1490	
  1491	Planning and document-only beads are valid execution targets — any bead
  1492	with unmet acceptance criteria and no blocking deps is eligible.
  1493	
  1494	Close semantics (per execute-bead result status):
  1495	  success                      — close bead with session + commit evidence
  1496	  already_satisfied            — close bead (after repeated no_changes)
  1497	  no_changes                   — unclaim; may cooldown or close after retries
  1498	  land_conflict                — unclaim; result preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/
  1499	  post_run_check_failed        — unclaim; result preserved
  1500	  execution_failed             — unclaim
  1501	  structural_validation_failed — unclaim
  1502	
  1503	Only success (and already_satisfied) closes the bead. Every other status
  1504	leaves the bead open and unclaimed so a later attempt can try again. Each
  1505	attempt is appended to the bead as an execute-bead event (status, detail,
  1506	base_rev, result_rev, preserve_ref, retry_after), and the underlying agent
  1507	session log is recorded under the execute-bead agent-log path.
  1508	
  1509	execute-loop runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no
  1510	separate "submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but
  1511	ignored (deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
  1512	
  1513	Project targeting (multi-project servers):
  1514	  --project <path>    target a specific project root (absolute path or name)
  1515	  DDX_PROJECT_ROOT    env var fallback; used when --project is not set
  1516	  (default)           the git root of the current working directory
  1517	
  1518	When submitting to a multi-project server you must ensure the target project
  1519	is registered with the server (run "ddx server" from that directory, or use
  1520	"ddx server projects register"). The server rejects unrecognised project paths.
  1521	`,
  1522			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit (normal surface)
  1523	  ddx agent execute-loop
  1524	
  1525	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
  1526	  ddx agent execute-loop --profile default --once
  1527	
  1528	  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker
  1529	  ddx agent execute-loop --poll-interval 30s
  1530	
  1531	  # Force a specific harness/model for a debugging pass
  1532	  ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness codex
  1533	  ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7`,
  1534			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
  1535			RunE: f.runAgentExecuteLoop,
  1536		}
  1537		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
  1538		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
  1539		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness to use")
  1540		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model override")
  1541		cmd.Flags().String("profile", agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart")
  1542		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider name (e.g. vidar, openrouter); selects a named provider from config")
  1543		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog")
  1544		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
  1545		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
  1546		cmd.Flags().Duration("poll-interval", 30*time.Second, "Poll interval for continuous scanning; zero drains current ready work and exits (legacy opt-out). Default 30s keeps the worker alive across empty polls.")
  1547		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
  1548		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; execute-loop always runs inline (ADR-022)")
  1549		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "execute-loop always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
  1550		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (e.g. for doc-only beads or tight iteration loops)")
  1551		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness to use for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
  1552		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
  1553		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop the loop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited; subscription and local providers do not count")
  1554		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout (e.g. 60m, 2h); overrides project config and model-class defaults. Use when a thinking model needs more than the default 15 min.")
  1555		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
  1556		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
  1557		return cmd
  1558	}
  1559	
  1560	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteLoop(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
  1561		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, false, "")
  1562	}
  1563	
  1564	// hostnameOrEmpty returns os.Hostname() or "" on error. Used to populate
  1565	// the workerprobe identity envelope without short-circuiting probe startup
  1566	// on a transient hostname-lookup failure.
  1567	func hostnameOrEmpty() string {
  1568		h, err := os.Hostname()
  1569		if err != nil {
  1570			return ""
  1571		}
  1572		return h
  1573	}
  1574	
  1575	// runAgentExecuteLoopImpl is the shared implementation for runAgentExecuteLoop
  1576	// and runWork. When treatPassthroughAsOpaque is true (ddx work path),
  1577	// harness/provider/model are passed to Execute unchanged and
  1578	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
  1579	// branch on their values. When false (ddx agent execute-loop path), the
  1580	// pre-flight validation gate runs first.
  1581	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd *cobra.Command, treatPassthroughAsOpaque bool, tryTargetBeadID string) error {
  1582		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
  1583		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
  1584		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
  1585		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
  1586		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
  1587		profile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("profile")
  1588		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
  1589		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
  1590		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
  1591		once, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("once")
  1592		pollInterval, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("poll-interval")
  1593		if tryTargetBeadID != "" {
  1594			// `ddx try <bead-id>` is a single-bead one-shot: target one bead and
  1595			// exit after one attempt regardless of CLI flag state.
  1596			once = true
  1597		}
  1598		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
  1599		noReview, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review")
  1600		reviewHarness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-harness")
  1601		reviewModel, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-model")
  1602		maxCostUSD, _ := cmd.Flags().GetFloat64("max-cost")
  1603		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
  1604		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
  1605		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
  1606	
  1607		// Zero-config auto-route: when the operator passes no routing flags and
  1608		// the project has no .ddx/config.yaml routing block, dispatch flows
  1609		// through fizeau's provider resolution. Provider configuration and any
  1610		// "no providers configured" error reporting is fizeau's concern; ddx
  1611		// does not read fizeau's config files.
  1612		//
  1613		// ddx-b2c9a245: tier is inferred per bead via escalation.InferTier in
  1614		// the executor closure below when no routing flags are present.
  1615		noRoutingFlags := harness == "" && model == "" && provider == "" && modelRef == "" &&
  1616			!cmd.Flags().Changed("profile")
  1617		autoInferTier := noRoutingFlags && !projectHasRoutingConfig(projectRoot)
  1618	
  1619		// ADR-022: execute-loop always runs inline. The legacy --local flag is
  1620		// a deprecated no-op and the server-submit path is removed from this CLI
  1621		// surface — server-spawned workers exec `ddx work` directly.
  1622	
  1623		// Pre-flight: validate harness availability and model compatibility
  1624		// before claiming any beads. This surfaces errors like "claude binary
  1625		// not found" or "vidar is an agent preset, not a claude model" before
  1626		// any bead status changes hands.
  1627		// Skipped for ddx work (treatPassthroughAsOpaque=true): harness/provider/model
  1628		// are opaque passthrough constraints there; DDx does not validate them.
  1629		if !treatPassthroughAsOpaque {
  1630			if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(cmd.Context(), f.WorkingDir, harness, model, provider, modelRef); err != nil {
  1631				return fmt.Errorf("execute-loop: %w", err)
  1632			}
  1633		}
  1634	
  1635		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
  1636	
  1637		// Structured progress sink for this loop run. Events emitted at
  1638		// loop.start, bead.claimed, bead.result, and loop.end land here so
  1639		// log aggregators (FormatSessionLogLines, `ddx server workers log`)
  1640		// can parse the same JSONL envelope used by harness session logs.
  1641		loopSessionID := fmt.Sprintf("agent-loop-%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
  1642		loopLogDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, agent.DefaultLogDir)
  1643		_ = os.MkdirAll(loopLogDir, 0o755)
  1644		loopLogPath := filepath.Join(loopLogDir, loopSessionID+".jsonl")
  1645		var loopSink io.Writer
  1740	
  1741		// singleTierAttempt runs one execution attempt with an explicit harness
  1742		// and model. It is called both by the non-escalating path and by each
  1743		// iteration of the tier escalation loop.
  1744		singleTierAttempt := func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, tier escalation.ModelTier, resolvedHarness, resolvedProvider, resolvedModel string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
  1745			gitOps := &agent.RealGitOps{}
  1746			attemptProvider := provider
  1747			if resolvedProvider != "" {
  1748				attemptProvider = resolvedProvider
  1749			}
  1750	
  1751			// ddx-b2c9a245: when zero-config auto-route is active, derive the
  1752			// per-attempt profile from the inferred tier rather than the static
  1753			// "always cheap" fallback the previous hot-fix used.
  1754			attemptProfile := profile
  1755			if autoInferTier && tier != "" {
  1756				attemptProfile = escalation.TierToProfile(tier)
  1757			}
  1758	
  1759			loopOverrides := config.CLIOverrides{
  1760				Harness:           resolvedHarness,
  1761				Model:             resolvedModel,
  1762				Provider:          attemptProvider,
  1763				ModelRef:          modelRef,
  1764				Profile:           attemptProfile,
  1765				Effort:            effort,
  1766				MinPower:          minPower,
  1767				MaxPower:          maxPower,
  1768				OpaquePassthrough: treatPassthroughAsOpaque,
  1769			}
  1770			if requestTimeout > 0 {
  1771				loopOverrides.ProviderRequestTimeout = &requestTimeout
  1772			}
  1773			attemptRcfg, _ := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, loopOverrides)
  1774			res, execErr := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, attemptRcfg, agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
  1775				FromRev:    fromRev,
  1776				BeadEvents: bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
  1777			}, gitOps)
  1778			if execErr != nil && res == nil {
  1779				return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, execErr
  1780			}
  1781			if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
  1782				targetBead, _ := store.Get(beadID)
  1783				landRes, _, landErr := agent.SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
  1784					ctx, projectRoot, targetBead, res,
  1785					func(req agent.LandRequest) (*agent.LandResult, error) { return localCoord.Submit(req) },
  1930					len(d.SkippedEpics), strings.Join(d.SkippedEpics, ", "))
  1931			}
  1932			if len(d.SkippedOnCooldown) > 0 {
  1933				retryHint := ""
  1934				if d.NextRetryAfter != "" {
  1935					retryHint = " (next retry-after: " + d.NextRetryAfter + ")"
  1936				}
  1937				fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "  skipped %d bead(s) on retry cooldown%s: %s\n",
  1938					len(d.SkippedOnCooldown), retryHint, strings.Join(d.SkippedOnCooldown, ", "))
  1939			}
  1940			if len(d.SkippedNotEligible) > 0 {
  1941				fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "  skipped %d bead(s) with execution-eligible=false: %s\n",
  1942					len(d.SkippedNotEligible), strings.Join(d.SkippedNotEligible, ", "))
  1943			}
  1944			if len(d.SkippedSuperseded) > 0 {
  1945				fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "  skipped %d superseded bead(s): %s\n",
  1946					len(d.SkippedSuperseded), strings.Join(d.SkippedSuperseded, ", "))
  1947			}
  1948			return nil
  1949		}
  1950	
  1951		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "\nproject: %s\n", projectRoot)
  1952		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "completed: %d  |  successes: %d  |  failures: %d\n", result.Attempts, result.Successes, result.Failures)
  1953		if result.Failures > 0 {
  1954			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "\nfailed:\n")
  1955			for _, attempt := range result.Results {
  1956				if attempt.Status != "success" {
  1957					fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "  - %s: %s", attempt.BeadID, attempt.Detail)
  1958					if attempt.PreserveRef != "" {
  1959						fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), " (preserved)")
  1960					}
  1961					fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout())
  1962				}
  1963			}
  1964		}
  1965		return nil
  1966	}
  1967	
  1968	// resolveProjectRoot returns the project root to use for execute-loop,
  1969	// execute-bead, and agent-run commands. Resolution order:
  1970	//  1. --project flag value (if non-empty)
  1971	//  2. DDX_PROJECT_ROOT environment variable
  1972	//  3. CWD-based git project root detection (gitpkg.FindProjectRoot)
  1973	func resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, workingDir string) string {
  1974		if projectFlag != "" {
  1975			return projectFlag
  1976		}
  1977		if env := os.Getenv("DDX_PROJECT_ROOT"); env != "" {
  1978			return env
  1979		}
  1980		return gitpkg.FindProjectRoot(workingDir)
  1981	}
  1982	
  1983	// buildCLIPreClaimHook returns a PreClaimHook for the inline execute-loop
  1984	// that fetches origin and verifies ancestry before each bead claim. Fetch
  1985	// failures are logged but do not block the worker (air-gap friendly).
  1986	func buildCLIPreClaimHook(projectRoot string, gitOps agent.LandingGitOps) func(ctx context.Context) error {
  1987		return func(ctx context.Context) error {
  1988			branch, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(projectRoot)
  1989			if err != nil {
  1990				return nil // can't determine branch — skip
  1991			}
  1992			res, err := gitOps.FetchOriginAncestryCheck(projectRoot, branch)
  1993			if err != nil {
  1994				return nil // fetch failure is non-fatal
  1995			}
  1996			if res.Action == "diverged" {
  1997				return fmt.Errorf("local branch %s has diverged from origin (local=%s origin=%s); reconcile manually before claiming",
  1998					branch, res.LocalSHA, res.OriginSHA)
  1999			}
  2000			return nil
  2001		}
  2002	}
  2003	
  2004	// resolveServerURL determines the base URL for the running DDx server.
  2005	//
  2006	// Resolution order (matches internal/serverreg):
  2007	//  1. DDX_SERVER_URL environment variable (explicit override)
  2008	//  2. ~/.local/share/ddx/server.addr (written by `ddx server` on startup)
  2009	//  3. https://127.0.0.1:7743 (the canonical default — see FEAT-020)
  2010	//
  2011	// The addr file may record a bind-address URL like https://0.0.0.0:7743
  2012	// (because the server binds to 0.0.0.0 for multi-interface access). Those
  2013	// are valid listen addresses but not reachable as client targets on some
  2014	// platforms — rewrite them to https://127.0.0.1:<port> for local clients.
  2015	//

data.text: I found one important current-state detail while gathering line evidence: the CLI text now says ADR-022 removed the server-submit path and `--local` is a deprecated no-op. That makes implementation even less suitable for a single pass, but the execute-bead protocol still calls for decomposition, so the child beads will keep that architectural conflict explicit where it matters.

create "agent: introduce canonical ExecuteLoopSpec package" "PROBLEM
Execute-loop execution fields are duplicated across cobra extraction, REST/GraphQL request structs, server ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec persistence, and runWorker consumption. Adding a flag can silently drop it on one transport.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 defines ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec as a server-local struct while cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 extracts a wider flag set, cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 has another request struct, and cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-75 has a third. The type boundary is not shared.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small import-safe package under cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ that defines ExecuteLoopSpec with JSON tags and fields ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Once, PollInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, and SpecVersion.
- Add DispatchOptions with only Local and JSON control-plane fields; do not persist it.
- Add Duration JSON support that marshals as strings and unmarshals both strings and numeric nanoseconds.
- Add ApplyDefaults and Validate methods, including SpecVersion support and a code comment documenting the single-version client/server forward-compat stance.
- Do not wire cobra, REST, GraphQL, or runWorker callers in this bead; later children consume this package.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not rename CLI flags or change LabelFilter from string.
- Do not change execute-loop validation policy beyond moving existing checks behind the spec lifecycle.
- Do not modify server or cobra call sites except imports needed by tests." "1. cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go defines ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, SupportedSpecVersion, ApplyDefaults(), and Validate().
2. ExecuteLoopSpec includes every field named in ddx-29058e2a AC #1, including SpecVersion, FromRev, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, and MaxPower.
3. TestExecuteLoopSpec_DurationJSONUsesStringAndAcceptsNumeric verifies PollInterval and RequestTimeout marshal as strings such as '"\\\"30s\\\" and unmarshal legacy numeric nanoseconds.
4. TestExecuteLoopSpec_ApplyDefaultsAndValidateVersion verifies default SpecVersion/PollInterval behavior and rejects SpecVersion greater than SupportedSpecVersion with an error that exposes the supported capability/version.
5. TestExecuteLoopSpec_DispatchOptionsNotPersisted verifies marshaling ExecuteLoopSpec never includes local or json fields.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/executeloop/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.\"

create \"cmd: parse execute-loop flags into ExecuteLoopSpec\" \"PROBLEM
The execute-loop/work CLI path still extracts each flag into local variables and threads those values through bespoke inline logic. The root bead requires a single cobra-to-spec conversion so execution fields are not re-maintained at every boundary.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 reads ProjectRoot/from/routing/review/cost/timeout/power flags into local variables, then cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1759-1777 manually projects a subset into config.CLIOverrides and ExecuteBeadRuntime. Control-plane flags are mixed with execution fields in the same RunE body.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd, treatPassthroughAsOpaque) returning (executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec, executeloop.DispatchOptions, error).
- Make runAgentExecuteLoopImpl call parseExecuteLoopSpec once, then use the spec for inline execution inputs and the dispatch options for --json/--local behavior.
- Keep current ADR-022 behavior: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1509-1511 says execute-loop always runs inline and --local is a deprecated no-op. If a live HTTP submit helper still exists, make it accept and marshal ExecuteLoopSpec directly; otherwise do not resurrect server-submit mode.
- Preserve ddx try behavior: tryTargetBeadID still forces Once true and FromRev still reaches ExecuteBeadRuntime.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change route validation semantics or auto-infer tier behavior.
- Do not change server REST/GraphQL handlers; separate children own those transports.
- Do not reshape LabelFilter.\" \"1. parseExecuteLoopSpec returns (executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec, executeloop.DispatchOptions, error) and is the only code in cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go that calls cmd.Flags().Get* for execute-loop/work execution flags.
2. DispatchOptions contains --json and --local only; ExecuteLoopSpec JSON does not contain those control-plane fields.
3. runAgentExecuteLoopImpl uses spec.ProjectRoot, spec.Harness, spec.Model, spec.Profile, spec.Provider, spec.ModelRef, spec.Effort, spec.Once, spec.PollInterval, spec.NoReview, spec.ReviewHarness, spec.ReviewModel, spec.MaxCostUSD, spec.RequestTimeout, spec.MinPower, spec.MaxPower, spec.OpaquePassthrough, and spec.FromRev instead of parallel locals.
4. TestParseExecuteLoopSpec_AllFlags verifies non-default values for every execute-loop flag land in ExecuteLoopSpec or DispatchOptions as appropriate.
5. TestRunAgentExecuteLoop_FromRevAndPowerUseParsedSpec verifies FromRev, MinPower, MaxPower, RequestTimeout, and OpaquePassthrough reach ExecuteBeadRuntime/config overrides on the inline path.
6. Existing local/ADR-022 behavior remains: --local is accepted as a no-op and does not submit to the server.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.\"

create \"server: decode REST execute-loop workers from ExecuteLoopSpec\" \"PROBLEM
The REST worker start endpoint drops execution fields because it decodes into a handler-local request struct that is narrower than the CLI/runtime spec.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 defines a private request struct missing opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power, from_rev, and spec_version. cli/internal/server/server.go:2441-2455 then manually constructs ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec, creating another silent omission point.

PROPOSED FIX
- Decode POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop directly into executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec.
- Resolve ProjectRoot using existing resolveRequestedProject behavior, write it back to the spec, then call ApplyDefaults and Validate before StartExecuteLoop.
- Add a shared server-side start helper if needed so REST and GraphQL use the same ApplyDefaults -> Validate -> StartExecuteLoop lifecycle.
- Return HTTP 400 for unsupported future SpecVersion with a JSON capability list; keep unknown JSON fields tolerated for newer-server/older-client compatibility.
- Preserve existing 403 trust check and 422 unknown project behavior.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not rework HTTP transport or endpoint path.
- Do not modify GraphQL dispatch; a separate child owns that parallel path.
- Do not change worker runtime consumption beyond what is necessary to pass the unified spec to StartExecuteLoop.\" \"1. The handler-local request struct at cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 is deleted; handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes into executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec.
2. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker calls ApplyDefaults and Validate before StartExecuteLoop, after resolving ProjectRoot.
3. TestStartExecuteLoopWorkerHTTP_DecodesUnifiedSpec verifies opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power, from_rev, and spec_version survive the HTTP request and are visible in the StartExecuteLoop spec.
4. TestStartExecuteLoopWorkerHTTP_RejectsFutureSpecVersion verifies SpecVersion greater than SupportedSpecVersion returns HTTP 400 with supported capability/version fields.
5. TestExecuteLoopProjectRootViaHTTP continues to pass and still rejects unregistered project roots with the existing 422 behavior.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'TestStartExecuteLoopWorkerHTTP|TestExecuteLoopProjectRootViaHTTP' green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.\"

create \"server: route GraphQL worker dispatch through ExecuteLoopSpec\" \"PROBLEM
GraphQL worker dispatch has the same parallel-struct smell as REST, so fixing only the REST handler would leave another transport able to drop future execute-loop fields.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-75 defines a GraphQL-local request struct and cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127-141 manually constructs ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec. It also parses poll_interval separately from REST, bypassing a shared spec lifecycle.

PROPOSED FIX
- Decode rawArgs directly into executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec.
- Preserve workers.default_spec behavior from cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:83-104 by applying defaults onto the unified spec before server-side ApplyDefaults/Validate.
- Route GraphQL through the same server-side helper used by REST so ApplyDefaults, Validate, SpecVersion handling, ProjectRoot assignment, and StartExecuteLoop are not reimplemented.
- Delete the GraphQL-local request struct and the parallel StartExecuteLoop literal.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change GraphQL schema or worker kind names.
- Do not change workers.max_count semantics.
- Do not change REST handler behavior; that belongs to the REST child.\" \"1. cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go no longer contains a GraphQL-local execute-loop request struct or a manual ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal.
2. workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker decodes rawArgs into executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec and invokes the same helper path as REST before StartExecuteLoop.
3. TestWorkerDispatchAdapter_UnifiedSpecCarriesAllFields verifies rawArgs fields opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power, from_rev, spec_version, and poll_interval reach the StartExecuteLoop spec.
4. Existing TestWorkerDispatchAdapter_DefaultProfileWhenNoArgs and TestWorkerDispatchAdapter_WorkersDefaultSpecPreservesEmptyProfile are updated to assert default-profile behavior through ExecuteLoopSpec.
5. TestWorkerDispatchAdapter_RejectsFutureSpecVersion verifies unsupported SpecVersion returns a GraphQL dispatch error carrying the supported capability/version.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'TestWorkerDispatchAdapter' green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.\"

create \"server: replace ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec with unified spec in workers\" \"PROBLEM
Worker persistence and runtime execution still use a server-local ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec, so fields present at cobra or wire boundaries can be lost before spec.json or runWorker consumes them.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 defines ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec without MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, or SpecVersion. cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-845 builds CLIOverrides and ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime from that incomplete spec, so runWorker cannot honor those fields on server-spawned workers.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec with executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec, or make ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec a temporary type alias to executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec if needed for a mechanical migration.
- Persist the fully resolved unified spec to .ddx/workers/<id>/spec.json after ApplyDefaults has already run.
- Update lifecycleStartDetail, StartExecuteLoop, buildRoutePreflight, runWorker, and test literals to consume the unified spec.
- Ensure runWorker passes MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, OpaquePassthrough, and FromRev into config/loop/runtime paths, with escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD used only as the zero-value fallback.
- Keep disk reads tolerant of unknown legacy fields such as min_tier and max_tier.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change queue picking, route preflight policy, landing behavior, or watchdog behavior.
- Do not implement REST/GraphQL decode changes here except compile fixes.
- Do not change LabelFilter semantics.\" \"1. cli/internal/server/workers.go no longer owns an independent ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct; any compatibility name is an alias to executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec.
2. StartExecuteLoop persists spec.json using the unified struct after defaults are applied.
3. runWorker consumes spec.MaxCostUSD, spec.RequestTimeout, spec.MinPower, spec.MaxPower, spec.OpaquePassthrough, and spec.FromRev; escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD appears only in the explicit fallback when MaxCostUSD is zero.
4. TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection uses reflection over exported ExecuteLoopSpec fields and proves each non-zero field survives JSON marshal/unmarshal, spec.json persistence, and the runWorker-visible spec.
5. TestWorkerExecuteLoopSpec_LoadsLegacySpecJSON verifies .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json loads without error while ignoring min_tier, max_tier, and other unknown fields.
6. TestWorker_NoPreflight_WhenOpaquePassthrough and a new TestWorker_OpaquePassthroughSkipsStartAndRoutePreflight assert both validation gates at workers.go:357-360 and workers.go:622-624 are skipped.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.\"

create \"agent: update reachability roots for ExecuteLoopSpec package\" \"PROBLEM
Adding cli/internal/agent/executeloop creates a new production entry package that the reachability checker must understand, or later REACH-PROTO work can incorrectly report the new spec code as unreachable.

ROOT CAUSE
The parent bead explicitly notes REACH-PROTO ddx-a946c744 must know about the new cli/internal/agent/executeloop package as an entry root. Without a tracker slice, the implementation children can land a new package before the reachability rules are updated.

PROPOSED FIX
- Locate the production-reachability checker configuration or entry-root list introduced by ddx-a946c744.
- Add cli/internal/agent/executeloop as an allowed production entry root once the canonical package exists.
- Add or update the checker test that proves the package is reachable/accepted.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not modify ExecuteLoopSpec behavior.
- Do not change unrelated reachability rules.
- If ddx-a946c744 has not landed when this bead runs, write a no_changes_rationale with status: needs_investigation naming that dependency instead of guessing the final checker location.\" \"1. The reachability checker entry-root configuration includes cli/internal/agent/executeloop after the spec package exists.
2. TestReachability_AllowsExecuteLoopSpecPackage (or the existing reachability test name if different) proves cli/internal/agent/executeloop is not flagged as unreachable production code.
3. If the checker from ddx-a946c744 is absent, .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/no_changes_rationale.txt uses status: needs_investigation and names ddx-a946c744 as the missing dependency.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/... -run 'Reach|reach|ExecuteLoopSpec' green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.\"
EOF"

ID:       ddx-1a9cc01f
Title:    agent: introduce canonical ExecuteLoopSpec package
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   phase:2, area:agent, area:server, kind:refactor, prevention, triage:needs-investigation
Parent:   ddx-29058e2a
Deps:     ddx-29058e2a
Desc:     PROBLEM
Execute-loop execution fields are duplicated across cobra extraction, REST/GraphQL request structs, server ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec persistence, and runWorker consumption. Adding a flag can silently drop it on one transport.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 defines ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec as a server-local struct while cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 extracts a wider flag set, cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 has another request struct, and cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-75 has a third. The type boundary is not shared.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small import-safe package under cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ that defines ExecuteLoopSpec with JSON tags and fields ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Once, PollInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, and SpecVersion.
- Add DispatchOptions with only Local and JSON control-plane fields; do not persist it.
- Add Duration JSON support that marshals as strings and unmarshals both strings and numeric nanoseconds.
- Add ApplyDefaults and Validate methods, including SpecVersion support and a code comment documenting the single-version client/server forward-compat stance.
- Do not wire cobra, REST, GraphQL, or runWorker callers in this bead; later children consume this package.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not rename CLI flags or change LabelFilter from string.
- Do not change execute-loop validation policy beyond moving existing checks behind the spec lifecycle.
- Do not modify server or cobra call sites except imports needed by tests.
Accept:   1. cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go defines ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, SupportedSpecVersion, ApplyDefaults(), and Validate().
2. ExecuteLoopSpec includes every field named in ddx-29058e2a AC #1, including SpecVersion, FromRev, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, and MaxPower.
3. TestExecuteLoopSpec_DurationJSONUsesStringAndAcceptsNumeric verifies PollInterval and RequestTimeout marshal as strings such as "30s" and unmarshal legacy numeric nanoseconds.
4. TestExecuteLoopSpec_ApplyDefaultsAndValidateVersion verifies default SpecVersion/PollInterval behavior and rejects SpecVersion greater than SupportedSpecVersion with an error that exposes the supported capability/version.
5. TestExecuteLoopSpec_DispatchOptionsNotPersisted verifies marshaling ExecuteLoopSpec never includes local or json fields.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/executeloop/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

197:{"acceptance":"1. Protocol spec: .ddx/checks/*.yaml schema documented; Go types in cli/internal/checks/protocol.go match the spec; JSON output schema documented.
2. Loader reads all .ddx/checks/*.yaml at runtime; invalid YAML or unknown fields fail loudly with file:line.
3. Runner invokes the configured command with documented env vars; reads ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json; treats non-zero exit as status=error.
4. ddx try integration: pre_merge hook runs ALL applicable checks (filtered by applies_to) in parallel before merge-back; any status=block or status=error aborts the merge and preserves the worktree per existing iteration-preservation conventions.
5. checks_bypass annotation on the bead skips the named check and records an event with the reason; missing reason = bypass rejected.
6. ddx ac run <bead-id> standalone subcommand runs the same checks against the current worktree state and prints results; exit 1 on any block/error.
7. End-to-end test: a fixture .ddx/checks/dummy-fail.yaml with a script that always returns status=block. Run ddx try on a no-op bead; observe the merge is aborted, worktree preserved, evidence file written, event recorded.
8. End-to-end test: same fixture + checks_bypass annotation on the bead → merge proceeds, bypass event recorded.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/checks/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Evidence under .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ shows the check fixture wrote its result.json and ddx parsed it correctly.","closing_commit_sha":"21696af5753c22de0af06641243a30de3900e1c6","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:34:44.62487483Z","description":"Add a language-agnostic check protocol to ddx so projects can declare mechanical pre-merge gates that ddx try evaluates before any worktree is merged back. Solves the recurring "implemented but not wired" failure mode at the platform layer rather than per-project.

CONTEXT
- Audit at /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md surfaced 4 functions with tests but no production caller; reviewer didn't catch it because the diff "looked right" and tests passed.
- ac_structural_check.go (cli/internal/agent/) is the existing seed: parses claims from AC prose for test_name/file_deleted/field_removed. It can't catch the unwired case because a unit test naming the function satisfies the claim vacuously.
- Prior plan considered embedding Go-specific call-graph analysis directly in cli/internal/agent/. Rejected as a boundary violation (CLAUDE.md: "DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions"). Each language ecosystem has its own dead-code tooling (Go: deadcode/callgraph; Python: vulture; TS: ts-prune/knip; Rust: cargo-udeps).

DESIGN — protocol (declarative, plugin-based)
- Discovery: .ddx/checks/*.yaml each declares one check: { name, command, when: pre_merge, applies_to: { paths: [globs] | always, labels: [...] } }
- Inputs to check command (env vars): BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID
- Output contract: check writes ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with schema { status: pass|block|error, message: string, violations: [{file, line, symbol, kind, detail}] }
- Exit code 0 = ran-to-completion (status field is authoritative); non-zero = check itself errored (treated as BLOCK with status=error)
- Hook point: pre_merge — runs in the parent (after worker's worktree work is done, BEFORE the merge-back into integration branch). BLOCK aborts the merge; the worktree stays preserved per existing conventions for human resolution.
- Bypass: bead-level annotation checks_bypass: [{name: <check>, reason: <text>, bead: <bead-id>}] recorded in events. No CLI flag — bypass must be tracked.
- Standalone: ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] runs the same checks locally so devs can iterate without spawning a full try attempt.

WHY pre_merge (NOT pre_close in the worker)
- User directive: "We can't afford to merge this kind of dead code." Worker-side checks could be bypassed by a worker that ignores BLOCK; parent-side gate cannot.
- Worker still gets feedback via ddx ac run for local iteration.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Multiple lifecycle hook points beyond pre_merge (add later if needed).
- Check dependencies / ordering (independent + parallel until proven insufficient).
- Plugin manifests, capability flags, version negotiation.
- Auto-discovery beyond .ddx/checks/*.yaml.
- Retry / timeout policy (project owns; checks should be fast).
- Any specific language check (separate beads).

OUT OF SCOPE: REACH-GO (the Go-specific deadcode-based check) and REACH-BACKFILL (the systematic application across cli/) are separate beads that depend on this one.

FILES (anticipated)
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — config schema + JSON I/O types
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — subprocess invocation, env injection, output parsing, evidence dir mgmt
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — wire pre_merge call before merge step; BLOCK aborts merge
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run subcommand
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for protocol contract","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","fallback_chain":[],"requested_harness":"claude"}","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:51:35.10675671Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260502T204932-0e555908","harness":"claude","input_tokens":16,"output_tokens":7133,"total_tokens":7149,"cost_usd":0.6323640000000001,"duration_ms":120475,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:51:35.124392574Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=7149 cost_usd=0.6324"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"Bead ddx-a946c744 was decomposed into two child beads rather than implemented in one attempt. reason: |
  Bead ddx-c9ef6c4f ("Implement axon backend behind selectable backend flag")
  cannot be completed in a single execute-bead pass because two upstream
  prerequisites are not yet in place, and the AC as written requires a
  working end-to-end integration against an external Rust service. Forcing
  a commit here would land non-functional scaffolding (the failure mode
  the parent epic ddx-5d49b14e was already BLOCKED for).

  Specific gaps observed in the current worktree (base ab0a056a):

  1. Dependency bead ddx-bbdd7564 ("Introduce selectable bead Backend
     interface in cli/internal/bead") is still status=open. The current
     cli/internal/bead/backend.go defines only the legacy interface:

         Backend interface {
             Init() error
             ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
             WriteAll(beads []Bead) error
             WithLock(fn func() error) error
         }

     TD-030 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md
     §"Backend interface (Go)") explicitly says this interface is "too
     coarse for Axon — WriteAll(beads []Bead) cannot reasonably be
     implemented against a remote entity store" and prescribes a richer
     per-entity surface (Get/Create/Update with OCC Version,
     AppendEvent, AddDep/RemoveDep, Ready, Close, Export/Import,
     ErrVersionMismatch, etc.). Implementing the axon backend against
     the legacy 4-method interface would (a) violate TD-030 and (b)
     produce a backend that cannot honor claim/OCC semantics that the
     bead description and AC #3 require ("plus claim semantics and
     event append"). This bead's <description> says it "follows the TD
     authored in the design child" — that TD assumes the interface
     refactor has landed first.

  2. No Axon Go SDK or generated gRPC client exists in this repo.
     /home/erik/Projects/axon ships axon.proto at
     crates/axon-server/proto/axon.proto (377 lines, AxonService with
     CreateEntity/GetEntity/UpdateEntity/CommitTransaction/Traverse/
     QueryEntities/PutSchema/CreateCollection/etc.) but no Go bindings.
     TD-030 §"Wire transport" specifies generation via
     protoc-gen-go + protoc-gen-go-grpc into cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/
     with a `make gen-axon-client` target and a checked-in axon.proto
     plus sha256. None of that scaffolding exists; protoc toolchain
     setup, proto vendoring, and the make target are themselves a
     non-trivial chunk of work that should be its own bead.

  3. AC #5 ("ddx bead commands work end-to-end against the axon
     backend on a fresh repo, no migration required") cannot be
     verified inside this worktree. Per TD-030 §D1 the operator runs a
     separate axon-server process; per §D4 the backend refuses all ops
     when Axon is unreachable. There is no Axon server running in this
     execution environment and no fixture / test-double that the AC
     would accept ("end-to-end ... on a fresh repo" rules out an
     in-memory fake).

  Additional context: the parent epic ddx-5d49b14e was BLOCKED in
  post-merge review on 2026-05-02 for landing only execution evidence
  with no implementation, then decomposed into seven children. This
  bead is one of those children but was queued out of dependency order
  — ddx-bbdd7564 (the interface refactor child) needs to land first.

what_is_done:
  - Read TD-030 (the design child's output) end-to-end.
  - Confirmed current Backend interface in cli/internal/bead/backend.go
    is the legacy form, not the TD-030 form.
  - Confirmed dependency bead ddx-bbdd7564 is still open.
  - Confirmed no axon proto/client is vendored under cli/internal/bead/.

what_is_blocking:
  - ddx-bbdd7564 must land first to introduce the per-entity Backend
    interface (Get/Create/Update with Version/OCC, AppendEvent,
    AddDep/RemoveDep, Ready, Close, Export/Import, error sentinels)
    that this bead's axon implementation needs to satisfy.
  - A separate bead should establish the gRPC client generation
    pipeline: vendor cli/internal/bead/axon/proto/axon.proto from the
    axon repo (with sha256 pin), add the make gen-axon-client target,
    add tooling docs for protoc-gen-go / protoc-gen-go-grpc, and
    commit the generated cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/ tree. This is
    explicitly called out by TD-030 §"Wire transport" but is not in
    the current bead's scope and has no existing bead covering it.
  - AC #5 needs a clarified verification path: either (a) require a
    running Axon test server in CI (with the deployment work that
    implies), or (b) loosen "end-to-end" to mean "round-trip against
    a vendored Axon test harness" and add the harness as a bead.

what_a_followup_would_need:
  - Re-queue this bead (ddx-c9ef6c4f) only after ddx-bbdd7564 closes
    with the TD-030-shaped Backend interface in place.
  - Add (and close) a new prerequisite bead for the axon gRPC client
    generation: vendor proto, wire `make gen-axon-client`, commit
    generated stubs under cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/.
  - Add a new prerequisite bead for the Axon test harness used by AC
    #5 (either a test-only docker-compose entry, a `go test`-managed
    server-under-test, or an explicit "skip if AXON_ADDR unset"
    integration-test gate per the project's existing test-tier
    conventions).
  - Then this bead becomes a focused implementation: wrap the
    generated client in cli/internal/bead/axon/, register the
    "axon" backend in cli/internal/bead/registry.go, plumb the
    DDX_BEAD_BACKEND / .ddx/config.yaml selector, and add the
    create/claim/close/dep unit tests AC #4 names (against the
    harness from the new prerequisite bead).
rationale: status: needs_investigation

Tracker bead for propagation of upstream ddx-agent fixes filed 2026-04-17/18.
Both fresh-eyes reviews (codex + opus, 2026-04-18) flagged the need for a
single consolidated tracker on the DDx side — this is it.

## Status table (refreshed 2026-04-19; DDx is now on agent v0.5.0)

| Upstream bead | Title                                 | Upstream status | DDx bump     | DDx commit  | DDx consumer / smoke                                                                                                       |
|---------------|---------------------------------------|-----------------|--------------|-------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| agent-92f0f324| DetectedFlavor                        | landed v0.3.12  | v0.3.11→12   | c9368312    | Covered by ddx-4817edfd (capability-gating epic; decomposed into 4 sub-beads, all closed).                                 |
| agent-767549c7| capability flags (SupportsTools)      | landed v0.3.12  | v0.3.11→12   | c9368312    | ddx-4817edfd sub-beads: context-window gating, tool-calling gating, effort/permissions gating, structured-output gating.   |
| agent-941e7e42| AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE                      | landed v0.3.12  | v0.3.11→12   | c9368312    | Used ad-hoc via scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh (regenerates fixtures); no CI-level regression test drives it.             |
| agent-04639431| strip thinking field on non-Anthropic | landed v0.3.13  | v0.3.12→13   | 9d057f83    | NO DDx CONSUMER NEEDED — the filter is entirely internal to agent's provider layer; DDx just sees the clean content stream.|
| agent-f237e07b| SSE comment-frame filter              | landed v0.3.14  | v0.3.13→14   | b59ebacf    | ddx-bbb2d177 smoke test landed in 1cd8e3a3 and was deleted in d5ebab2b during the v0.5.0 cutover (live-smoke equivalent now lives in the ddx-agent comparison/benchmark suite). Fixture files remain orphaned at cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/ and should be cleaned up in a follow-up. |
| agent-a365bcf2| preset rename (cheap/default/smart)   | in_progress     | —            | —           | Blocks this bead's closure. When it lands, file a DDx migration bead for any --preset references in .ddx/config.yaml (currently at least one harness uses '--preset codex').|

## v0.5.0 cutover context (2026-04-19)

Commit d5ebab2b cut DDx over to agent v0.5.0 and dropped the local replace
directive. That post-dates the v0.3.x-era fixes this tracker was originally
scoped around. v0.5.0 contracted the public surface (provider/openai now
internal/), which is why the omlx smoke test was removed rather than
migrated. Any residual v0.3.x consumer test would be orphaned by the surface
contraction anyway — the DDx side of that consumer coverage now lives in the
upstream agent repo's benchmark suite, and DDx's own execute-bead path is
covered by the broader test set in cli/internal/agent/.

## Remaining work before this bead closes

1. Upstream agent-a365bcf2 (preset rename) lands.
2. File a DDx-side migration bead: sweep .ddx/config.yaml, library templates,
   and any hardcoded preset strings for claude/codex/cursor/agent aliases;
   swap to smart/cheap/default/minimal; optionally keep a compatibility shim
   for one release.
3. Optional (P4): delete the orphaned cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/
   fixtures (no test consumes them post-v0.5.0) OR relocate them to the
   upstream benchmark suite.
4. Close this tracker bead.

## Non-goals

- Re-implementing the upstream fixes. They've landed.
- Tracking ddx-agent's own backlog (agent-b1c2d3e4 manifest v4 etc.). That's upstream's concern.

1132:{"acceptance":"**User story:** After the upstream point release lands and DDx pulls it, `ddx agent run` with no flags just works on my machine. `--profile local`, `--harness claude`, `--model opus-4.7` behave per the upstream contract. The drain-queue failure mode (empty spec → gemini+minimax → 19-burn) is impossible.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Upstream prerequisite tagged and pulled.** Go.mod references a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class `default` and `local` profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs + gating; `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible`; public decision-trace (`RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error); cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` with unknown-cost fallback. All upstream beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` labeled `routing-plan` are closed.

2. **No DDx-side synthesis on default path.**
   - `ddx agent run` with zero flags issues exactly one `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call, no Model, no Harness, no Provider.
   - `ddx work` default: same shape.
   - `ddx agent execute-bead` with no overrides: same shape.
   - Integration test: same config that caused the drain-queue failure (real `~/.local/share/ddx/.ddx/config.yaml`) now either succeeds or returns a single typed error — never 19-burn.
   - Unit test: `workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker({})` → spec with `Profile: "default"`, no other fields. No `ResolveTierModelRef`, no `ResolveProfileLadder` in the call graph on the default path.

3. **Preflight before claim.**
   - Call sequence at `execute_bead_loop.go:352`: next-candidate → `ResolveRoute` → (preflight gate, new) → `Claim` → invoke. Route error OR preflight rejection exits the loop with a `worker-level` failure — no bead claimed.
   - Test: seed a pathological route (harness with allow-list that excludes the decision's model — fabricated via upstream test seam). Assert `beadStore.Claim` is never called; worker record shows `status: execution_failed` with detail naming the rejected pair; no bead has a `kind:tier-attempt` event from this attempt.

4. **Config deprecation is deliberate, not warning-only.**
   - Loading a config with `agent.routing.default_harness` set produces a **hard error** at startup with a migration pointer. Field is deleted from the config schema.
   - Loading a config with `agent.routing.profile_ladders` or `agent.routing.model_overrides` set produces a one-time warning per process AND causes the default path to ignore them. Opt-in with `--escalate` or `--override-model` explicit flags.
   - CHANGELOG entry + migration guide in DDx docs.
   - Migration test: fixture config containing all three deprecated fields, run `ddx agent run` with no flags, assert warnings printed and default path honored (not the deprecated config).

5. **Override behavior matches G3 exactly.**
   - `--harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Harness: "claude")`. If claude fails upstream's gates (binary missing, quota exhausted), surfaces the typed error; no silent substitution. E2E test mocks each gate failure.
   - `--model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "opus-4.7")`. Lowest-cost available quota-OK provider wins. E2E test seeds 3 providers at different cost with shared model; asserts lowest-cost pick.
   - `--harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` when combo is invalid; clean dispatch otherwise.
   - `--profile local --harness claude` → upstream typed error ("claude is non-local, profile=local is local-only"); DDx surfaces it unmodified. E2E test.

6. **Playwright — drain-queue goldens.**
   - Fixture matches user's real config (endpoints: lmstudio + omlx + lmstudio + lmstudio, no gemini binary test or with gemini installed but not in allow-list for the chosen model).
   - Click Drain on project home → within 5s: worker row appears with harness = `agent` (or another live-endpoint-backed harness). Never gemini + non-gemini model. Never 19 failures.
   - After drain completes: worker status `success` (or typed error exit with reason recorded on the bead). Attempts count matches actual work done.

7. **No compensating DDx logic retained.**
   - Search DDx for code that re-implements any of: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering, profile-to-preference mapping, provider cost scoring. Must be absent. If found, PR fails review.
   - `ResolveProfileLadder`, `ResolveTierModelRef`, `workersByHarness` escalation helpers are deleted from the default path. They remain reachable via `--escalate` only. Dead-code detector run in CI catches orphans.

8. **D6 / D7 are explicitly out of scope and filed as follow-ups.**
   - `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter) remains its own bead and may land before or after this one.
   - A new bead is filed post-release for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer work, pointing at A4 upstream as satisfied.

9. **Documentation.**
   - DDx's CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md links to upstream profile catalog + override precedence + best-provider contract docs. No re-documentation on the DDx side.
   - DDx CHANGELOG entry covers the go.mod bump, D4 breaking changes, and new CLI surface semantics.

10. **Regression goldens for the drain-queue bug.**
    - Integration test exists that reproduces the original failure mode (empty spec + user's config + gemini binary installed + minimax model in overrides). Test stays in the suite as a regression guard.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:26:11.505315747Z","description":"## Status after codex review (2026-04-22)

Original plan was consolidated in this bead but **reduced in scope** after codex's review surfaced that most of the compatibility and data-model work belongs upstream in ddx-agent. This bead is now the **DDx-side tracker only**. Upstream work is queued as separate beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` (prefix `agent-*`) and will land as a single tagged point release; nothing in DDx lands until that release is pulled.

## Goals (unchanged — user stated 2026-04-22)

**G1. Zero-interaction defaults.** `ddx agent run` and `ddx agent execute-bead` just work with no flags.

**G2. Usable profiles.** `--profile standard`, `--profile local` force clear, documented rules.

**G3. Usable overrides.** `--harness claude` forces claude. `--model opus-4.7` forces opus-4.7. Within requirements: available, quota OK, lowest cost.

## Upstream dependencies (tracked in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/)

This bead blocks on a single upstream point release that carries:

- Profile catalog with first-class `default`, `local`, `standard` (and any others DDx needs to expose) + documented provider-preference rules.
- Supported-models allow-list on routing-inputs `HarnessEntry`; gating rejects non-allowed models even when `ExactPinSupport: true`.
- Typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` surfaced from `Execute` so explicit `--harness X --model Y` conflicts fail fast with actionable detail.
- Public `RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error for "no viable candidate" with per-candidate rejection reasons — DDx's decision-trace consumers need this.
- Cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` (`CostUSDPer1kTokens`) + scoring tiebreak that prefers lower cost, with unknown-cost fallback, subscription-quota pricing, and near-quota demotion.
- Documentation for the profile catalog, override precedence, and "best provider" contract.

Once that release is tagged, DDx work below proceeds.

## DDx-side changes (reduced scope)

**D1. Dispatch sends a real spec.**

`workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker` treats empty input as `{Profile: "default"}` explicitly. Dashboard Drain button calls the same path. No DDx-side default synthesis beyond "profile=default if unset."

**D2. Stop synthesizing a tier ladder by default.**

Default `ddx agent run` / `ddx agent execute-bead` / `ddx work` path calls `agentlib.ResolveRoute(Profile: spec.Profile)` with no Model string unless `spec.Model` was explicitly given. No per-tier iteration, no `ResolveTierModelRef` lookup, no preference-order fallback. The tier-ladder machinery becomes opt-in via `--escalate` only.

**D3. Preflight gate (defensive only, post-upstream).**

After `ResolveRoute` returns, DDx verifies the decision's `(Harness, Model)` is not in the typed-incompatibility set surfaced by upstream. The compatibility data comes from upstream — DDx does **not** duplicate the allow-list. Insertion point: `execute_bead_loop.go:352`, **before** `Claim`. Route/preflight failure exits the loop with a worker-level failure record; no bead is claimed, no burn cycle.

**D4. Deprecate DDx-side routing config.**

Deliberate breaking change in the same release that pulls the upstream point release:
- `agent.routing.default_harness` — field deleted; upstream's profile model makes it redundant.
- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` — kept but gated behind explicit `--escalate` only. Not read on the default path. Warning at config-load if set without `--escalate` ever being used in the life of the process.
- `agent.routing.model_overrides` — same treatment as `profile_ladders`: explicit-opt-in only, warning otherwise.
- `agent.harness` (top-level) — kept, clarified as a **preference for tie-breaks**, not a default override.

Migration note published in DDx changelog.

**D5. CLI surface parity with goals.**

- `ddx agent run` with zero flags → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")`. No other DDx inputs.
- `ddx agent run --profile local` → pass through unchanged to upstream.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Harness: "claude")`. Upstream picks best provider within claude.
- `ddx agent run --model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "opus-4.7")`. Upstream picks lowest-cost available quota-OK provider.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` from upstream when incompatible; clean success otherwise.
- `ddx work` (drain) default → same as `ddx agent run` default.

## Explicitly dropped from this bead (per codex)

- **D6 — `ddx agent route-status` / providers UI decision-trace consumers.** Adjacent but not required. Files as a follow-up once A4 lands upstream. Hooks into `ddx-23978824` (providers UI).
- **D7 — `workersByProject` id/path filter.** Independent bug. Stays as `ddx-05b4cc9d`; not blocked by this bead and does not block this bead.

## Sequencing

1. **Upstream beads land and point release is tagged.** (Separate queue in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.)
2. DDx `go.mod` bumps to that release.
3. D1, D2, D3, D5 land together in one DDx PR — no compensating paths retained.
4. D4 lands with D1-D3-D5 or in the immediate follow-up PR. Migration warning + changelog.
5. **Then** D6 / `ddx-23978824` / sessions-billing-mode (`ddx-6904a90b`) work can consume the new decision-trace surface.

## Cross-references

- Upstream beads: see `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` under label `routing-plan`.
- Direct codex review feedback logged in bead notes below.
- Supersedes original 3-step proposal from conversation history.
- Addresses `project_endpoint_routing_design.md` memory directive (2026-04-21): "kill named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy model match."
- Hard downstream dependents (wait for this bead):
  - `ddx-b6cf025c` — drain-worker count control (default profile must mean something first)
  - `ddx-23978824` — providers UI (needs decision trace from A4)
  - `ddx-6904a90b` — sessions billing-mode (benefits from richer session.routing metadata)
- Independent of this bead: `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workers filter).

## Codex review — key corrections folded in

1. A3 confirmed by code inspection: `HarnessEntry` has no model-allow-list field; `engine.go:456` `resolveModel` returns `req.Model` directly when no provider discovery; `gating.go:88` only checks `ExactPinSupport`. Allow-list must be added to routing inputs/capabilities, not just "fix ExactPin semantics."
2. A4 bigger than originally written: public `RouteDecision` has no `Candidates` field; service drops rejection data on error. Needs typed error + public surface.
3. Earlier claim that codex/claude have `Models: nil` was wrong — that's DDx's local `harness_config.go`, not upstream's `internal/harnesses/registry.go`. Upstream has codex=[gpt-5.4], claude=[sonnet, opus, claude-sonnet-4-6]. Allow-list work is universal upstream.
4. Upstream catalog has no `default` or `local` profile today (models.yaml:195). Must be added before DDx materializes them.
5. D3 restricted to defensive-only; all compatibility logic belongs upstream.
6. G2 precedence conflict (`--profile local --harness claude`): resolved in upstream docs as explicit-pin-wins but upstream returns typed error if the pin violates the profile's hard constraint (e.g., local-only with non-local harness).
7. G3 cost needs explicit data model — no price field on ProviderEntry today.
8. D4 reframed as deliberate breaking change, not warning-only drift.
9. D6 + D7 dropped from epic scope.

rationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)

## Decision

Stopping without a commit. This bead's acceptance criteria describe a
multi-PR release-adoption epic, not a single bounded change. Attempting it
in one execute-bead pass would produce either (a) a partial commit that
fails most AC items, or (b) a sprawling change touching dispatch, config
schema, CLI surface, frontend Playwright fixtures, and CHANGELOG/docs
without the up-front design alignment the bead itself calls for.

## What is already done (verified in this worktree)

1. **Upstream routing-plan beads are all closed.** All six beads under
   label `routing-plan` in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` are in
   `closed` status:
     - agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware routing)
     - agent-191a74f9 (default + local + standard profiles)
     - agent-1f46cf22 (routing docs)
     - agent-53f38d95 (public decision trace)
     - agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)
     - agent-dfabb10b (typed errors)

2. **Their closing commits are in DDx's currently-pinned upstream tag.**
   `cli/go.mod` references `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.
   `git tag --contains` for each routing-plan closing commit returns
   v0.9.10 as the earliest containing tag. v0.9.23 (and v0.9.24 latest)
   transitively contain all of them. So the *binary surface* AC #1
   wants is already accessible via the pinned go.mod.

## What is NOT done — and why this can't be a one-shot pass

### AC #1 — CHANGELOG-as-released-narrative

AC #1 asks for "a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class
default and local profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs +
gating; ErrHarnessModelIncompatible; public decision-trace …; cost
dimension on ProviderEntry with unknown-cost fallback."

Reading `~/Projects/agent/CHANGELOG.md`:
  - v0.9.10 documents prompt-caching work, not the routing-plan work.
  - v0.9.11 documents ADR-006 override-as-failure-signal.
  - v0.9.12-v0.9.24 cover lucebox/vllm, conformance, corpus, terminal-bench,
    pi-runner panic. None of these releases bundles a coherent
    "routing point release" narrative.

The closing commits for the six beads landed scattered between tags as
side-quest work; none of those tags' release notes call out the
combined contract DDx now wants to consume. AC #1 effectively requires
either (a) an upstream re-release that publishes a "v0.9.25 — routing
point release" entry covering exactly those items, OR (b) DDx's bead
description must be relaxed to accept "the code is in v0.9.23, even if
no single CHANGELOG block summarizes it." Either resolution is a
governance decision, not a coding task this worktree can execute.

### AC #2-7 — DDx-side surface (D1-D5)

These criteria collectively require:
  - rewriting the worker dispatch path so the default issues exactly one
    `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call with no Model / no Harness /
    no Provider, removing all DDx-side ladder synthesis;
  - inserting a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before bead claim
    (`execute_bead_loop.go` ~line 341);
  - making `agent.routing.default_harness` a hard error at config load,
    `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides` opt-in via `--escalate`/`--override-model`,
    with one-time process warnings;
  - guaranteeing override flag combinations route through unchanged to
    upstream and surface upstream typed errors;
  - removing `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef` /
    `workersByHarness` escalation helpers from the default path with
    dead-code detection in CI;
  - adding an integration test that reproduces the historical 19-burn
    drain-queue bug AND new e2e tests covering each override
    combination (claude+opus, model-only, harness-only, profile+harness
    conflict);
  - adding Playwright drain-queue golden fixtures matching the user's
    real config;
  - updating CHANGELOG and docs.

This is on the order of 1-3k LOC delta across:
  - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go`
  - `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` (workerDispatchAdapter)
  - `cli/internal/config/*` (deprecated field schema)
  - `cli/cmd/agent_*.go` (override flag plumbing)
  - new tests (unit, integration, regression, Playwright)

That work spans D1-D5 in the bead's own sequencing list, which the bead
explicitly bundles into "one DDx PR" — but a PR of that size needs
architectural alignment with the user (especially around the breaking
config change in D4 and the dead-code detector in AC #7) before the
first line is written. execute-bead's contract is to satisfy the AC
deterministically, not to make architecture decisions.

### AC #8 — D6 / D7 follow-up beads

The bead requires that follow-up beads be filed:
  - one for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer,
  - acknowledgement that `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workersByProject filter)
    stays independent.

Filing beads requires `ddx bead create` invocations against the
project's tracker and a commit that mutates `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. This is
mechanical but should ride along with the substantive PR, not a no-op.

### AC #10 — drain-queue regression golden

A net-new integration test that reproduces the user's historical
19-burn failure with their real config. Designing this test
(particularly the gemini-binary-installed assertion, since CI may not
have gemini available) is itself a sub-bead.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

To make this bead landable in a single execute-bead pass, the bead
must be split into smaller beads, each of which is a well-defined PR:

1. **routing-bump** — bump `cli/go.mod` from v0.9.23 to whatever
   tag (v0.9.25?) the upstream cuts as the routing point release; add
   DDx CHANGELOG entry citing upstream release notes. Trivially
   satisfies AC #1 once upstream cuts the tag.

2. **routing-default-path** — D1 + D2 only. Worker dispatch + default
   `ddx agent run` and `ddx work` issue `ResolveRoute(Profile:
   "default")`. New unit tests. Does not touch config schema yet.

3. **routing-preflight-gate** — D3 only. Insert preflight before
   `Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~341`. Test with seeded
   incompatibility.

4. **routing-config-deprecation** — D4 only. Hard error on
   `default_harness`, opt-in warnings on others. Migration test +
   CHANGELOG breaking-change note.

5. **routing-overrides-e2e** — D5 + AC #5 e2e tests for each override
   combination.

6. **routing-drain-regression** — AC #10. Standalone regression test
   that reproduces the historical 19-burn bug.

7. **routing-cleanup** — AC #7 dead-code detector + removal of
   compensating logic.

8. **routing-frontend-goldens** — AC #6 Playwright fixtures.

9. **routing-followups** — AC #8 file the route-status / providers-UI
   bead.

Each of those is a clean execute-bead pass with provable AC. The
current bead's AC list is the union of all nine — too broad for one
attempt.

## Recommendation to the orchestrator

Reopen this bead with `helix-evolve` (or equivalent) to split it into
the nine sub-beads above, then queue them in order. The first three
(bump + default-path + preflight-gate) unblock the user's stated
goals (G1/G2/G3) in the smallest possible PR.

The reduced-scope language in the bead's own description ("DDx-side
tracker only", "blocks on a single upstream point release") suggests
this bead was meant as the EPIC, not the deliverable. Treating it as
the deliverable in execute-bead is what produced this no-op outcome.

rationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)

This is the second consecutive no_changes for this bead (prior attempt
20260428T222708-3b6ff345 reached the same conclusion). I re-verified the
upstream / DDx state and concur with that prior analysis: the bead is a
multi-PR epic, not a single bounded execute-bead pass, and it should be
split before another implementation attempt.

## What is verified done

1. All six upstream routing-plan beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` are
   `closed`:
     agent-0dafc7f0, agent-191a74f9, agent-1f46cf22,
     agent-53f38d95, agent-bab52778, agent-dfabb10b.

2. Their closing commits are reachable from the tag DDx already pins:
   `cli/go.mod` → `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.
   `git merge-base --is-ancestor <SHA> v0.9.23` succeeds for the
   closing commits of every routing-plan bead checked
   (db26108, b645280, 0bc2bf4, b85e90e). So the upstream binary
   surface AC #1 wants is already on the consumer's classpath; no
   `go.mod` bump is even required.

## Why this still cannot be a single-pass commit

The bead's AC enumerates 10 items spanning D1–D5 plus follow-ups:

  - D1+D2: rip out DDx-side tier-ladder synthesis from the default
    `ddx agent run` / `ddx work` / `ddx agent execute-bead` paths so
    each issues exactly one `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call —
    touches `workerDispatchAdapter`, `execute_bead_loop.go`,
    `agent_cmd.go`, `profile_ladder.go`, `serviceconfig.go`.
  - D3: insert a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before
    `beadStore.Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~352`.
  - D4: deliberate breaking config change — `default_harness` becomes
    a hard error at load; `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides`
    move to opt-in-only with one-time warnings, requires schema
    deletion + migration test + CHANGELOG breaking-change note.
  - D5: CLI override surface — flag plumbing + per-flag e2e tests
    covering harness-only, model-only, harness+model conflict, and
    profile+harness conflict.
  - AC #6: Playwright drain-queue golden matching the user's real
    endpoint config + a "no gemini + non-gemini-model fallback"
    assertion.
  - AC #7: search-and-delete pass for any DDx-side
    allow-list / exact-pin / cost-scoring re-implementation, plus a
    dead-code detector wired into CI to catch orphans of
    `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef`.
  - AC #8: file follow-up beads for D6 (route-status / providers UI)
    and reaffirm `ddx-05b4cc9d` independence.
  - AC #9: link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream docs and write
    DDx CHANGELOG entry.
  - AC #10: net-new regression integration test that reproduces the
    19-burn drain-queue failure mode with the user's exact config —
    requires fixture work (gemini binary presence, minimax override)
    that is itself a sub-bead.

Conservatively: 1–3k LOC across ~10 Go files plus frontend Playwright
fixtures plus CI wiring plus tracker mutations. Crucially D4 is a
deliberate breaking change to user-facing config schema; landing it
without explicit user alignment risks breaking real `.ddx.yml` files
in the wild. The bead description itself describes the work as "one
DDx PR" but the AC list is the union of nine independent
deliverables.

execute-bead's contract is to satisfy AC deterministically, not to
make architecture or breaking-change decisions across nine
sub-deliverables in one pass. A partial commit would fail most AC
items; a sprawling commit would couple breaking schema changes to
mechanical refactors and frontend fixtures, which is exactly the
shape of PR the bead's own sequencing tries to avoid.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

Split this bead into the nine smaller beads listed in the prior
attempt's rationale (`.ddx/executions/20260428T222708-3b6ff345/result.json`,
section "What a follow-up attempt needs"):

  1. routing-bump (trivial; AC #1 — though arguably already
     satisfied by v0.9.23)
  2. routing-default-path (D1 + D2)
  3. routing-preflight-gate (D3)
  4. routing-config-deprecation (D4 — breaking, needs explicit
     user sign-off in its own bead description)
  5. routing-overrides-e2e (D5 + AC #5)
  6. routing-drain-regression (AC #10)
  7. routing-cleanup (AC #7 dead-code detector)
  8. routing-frontend-goldens (AC #6)
  9. routing-followups (AC #8)

Recommendation: reopen this bead with helix-evolve to perform the
split, keep ddx-fdd3ea36 as the epic tracker, and queue beads 2 and 3
first — they unblock G1/G2/G3 with the smallest reasonable PR.

The reduced-scope language in the bead's own description ("DDx-side
tracker only", "blocks on a single upstream point release") confirms
this was meant as an EPIC, not a deliverable. Two consecutive
execute-bead passes have now reached the same conclusion; further
re-execution at higher model tiers will not change the outcome —
the blocker is bead shape, not capability.
