    /home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!document!driven!d!x/agent@v0.9.23/routing_errors.go:
      ErrHarnessModelIncompatible, ErrProfilePinConflict, ErrNoProfileCandidate,
      ErrUnknownProfile, ErrNoLiveProvider, DecisionWithCandidates interface
      with RouteCandidates() []RouteCandidate.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING THIS ATTEMPT
=================================
The bead is an epic-tracker, not a single deliverable. AC #2 through #10
collectively require — in one PR with no compensating paths retained — all of:

  D1. workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker rewrite + Drain-button parity.
  D2. Removal of default-path tier-ladder synthesis from
      ddx agent run / ddx agent execute-bead / ddx work; ResolveProfileLadder
      and ResolveTierModelRef gated behind --escalate only.
  D3. Preflight gate inserted before beadStore.Claim in execute_bead_loop.go
      (the bead points at line 352, which has already drifted — the actual
      Claim site is now line 341; the loop is 1052 lines and has substantial
      surrounding dispatch state to thread through).
  D4. Hard-error breaking change for `agent.routing.default_harness`;
      one-time warnings + default-path ignore for `profile_ladders` and
      `model_overrides`. Schema deletion in
      cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json. Config-load test fixtures.
  D5. CLI surface parity for ddx agent run (no flags / --profile / --harness /
      --model / combos), ddx work, ddx agent execute-bead.

  Plus tests demanded by the AC:
  - AC #2 integration test reproducing the original 19-burn drain failure
    against the user's real config shape.
  - AC #3 worker-level failure record assertion with seeded pathological route
    via an upstream test seam that does not currently exist on the DDx side.
  - AC #5 four end-to-end override-behavior tests (incl. cost-tiebreak
    seeded-providers test).
  - AC #6 Playwright golden for the drain-queue UI path
    (cli/internal/server/frontend); fixture must mirror user's real
    endpoint config.
  - AC #7 dead-code detector wired into CI catching orphaned ladder helpers.
  - AC #10 regression-guard integration test that fails before this work and
    succeeds after.

  Plus the directive in AC #7 — "Search DDx for code that re-implements any
  of: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering, profile-to-preference mapping,
  provider cost scoring. Must be absent. If found, PR fails review." —
  requires a deliberate audit + removal pass spanning at least:
      cli/internal/agent/harness_config.go
      cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go
      cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder_test.go
      cli/internal/config/config.go
      cli/internal/config/types.go
      cli/internal/config/routing_ladder_test.go
      cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json
      cli/internal/server/workers.go
      cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go
      cli/cmd/agent_run_profile_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (1052 lines)
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_runwithconfig_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_resolvers_e2e_test.go
      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_pin_propagation_test.go

This is a multi-thousand-line, multi-subsystem refactor with a hard
breaking-change config migration and a new Playwright golden. It cannot be
landed responsibly in a single execute-bead pass without a much narrower
sub-bead breakdown. Attempting it as one commit risks: half-finished tier-
ladder removal that leaves the default path in an undefined state; a config
schema break that hard-errors existing user configs without the migration
warnings actually firing; or merging a Playwright golden whose fixture
silently passes because the underlying dispatch path was not fully rewired.

The previous attempt already correctly scoped this bead to AC #1 only and
documented the upstream prerequisite. Doing more here without
decomposition would either produce a bad commit or violate
DOWITYTD on a bead whose own description acknowledges it is a
"DDx-side tracker only" for an upstream-driven epic.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT NEEDS
==================================
Decompose ddx-fdd3ea36 into independently shippable sub-beads, each
small enough to land in one execute-bead pass with focused tests:

  ddx-<new>-D1: workerDispatchAdapter + Drain button — empty-spec defaults to
                Profile:"default"; no other synthesis. Unit test +
                dashboard wiring. Closes AC #2 partially.

  ddx-<new>-D2a: Default path stops calling ResolveTierModelRef /
                 ResolveProfileLadder. Add --escalate flag that re-enables
                 them. Tests assert call graph. Closes AC #2 fully.

  ddx-<new>-D3: Preflight gate before Claim using upstream's typed
                ErrHarnessModelIncompatible. Targeted test using upstream
                test seam (file the upstream test-seam ask as a separate
                ddx-agent bead if missing). Closes AC #3.

  ddx-<new>-D4: Config breaking-change migration. Hard-error on
                default_harness; warn-and-ignore on profile_ladders/
                model_overrides; schema update; migration fixture tests;
                CHANGELOG migration guide. Closes AC #4.

  ddx-<new>-D5: CLI flag-surface parity tests for ddx agent run / work /
                execute-bead with the four override permutations from AC #5.
                Closes AC #5.

  ddx-<new>-Audit: Audit pass for AC #7 (compensating logic removal +
                   dead-code orphan detector in CI). Closes AC #7.

  ddx-<new>-Regression: AC #10 regression-guard integration test that
                        reproduces the 19-burn drain bug; AC #2's
                        integration sibling. Closes AC #10 (and validates
                        D1+D2+D3 together).

  ddx-<new>-Playwright: AC #6 frontend golden against the rewired Drain
                        path. Last in sequence — depends on D1+D2+D3 +
                        Regression. Closes AC #6.

  ddx-<new>-Docs: Cross-link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream profile
                  catalog + override precedence + best-provider contract
                  docs (AC #9 second half — CHANGELOG half is already in
                  CHANGELOG.md from the prior commit).

  ddx-<new>-D6-followup: Decision-trace consumers in route-status /
                         providers UI — explicitly out of scope per the
                         bead description but should be filed now so the
                         tracking exists (bead description's own AC #8
                         requires this).

ddx-fdd3ea36 should remain open as the umbrella tracker until all
sub-beads close, at which point AC #2..#10 can be re-verified end-to-end
in one final review pass.

NO CODE CHANGES IN THIS ATTEMPT.

rationale: Bead: ddx-fdd3ea36 — Adopt ddx-agent routing point release (DDx-side consumer epic)

Disposition: no_changes — this is an epic/tracker bead whose work has been
deliberately decomposed into nine child beads. Some are closed; four remain
open. The tracker cannot close until those four close. There is no
implementation work that belongs at the epic level itself.

================================================================================
What is done
================================================================================

Upstream prerequisite (AC #1):
  All six routing-plan beads in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/ are closed and merged
  into tagged ddx-agent releases (v0.9.10..v0.9.24):
    agent-191a74f9  profiles (default/local/standard)         closed
    agent-bab52778  supported-models allow-list + gating      closed
    agent-dfabb10b  ErrHarnessModelIncompatible / PinConflict closed
    agent-53f38d95  public RouteDecision.Candidates           closed
    agent-0dafc7f0  CostUSDPer1kTokens + cost tiebreak        closed
    agent-1f46cf22  routing documentation                     closed
  cli/go.mod pins github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23, which contains
  every routing-plan commit (verified via `git tag --contains <sha>` against
  closing_commit_sha for each upstream bead).

DDx-side child beads already closed:
  ddx-967e9802  routing-bump (go.mod pin + CHANGELOG)         closed
  ddx-755f5881  routing-default-path (D1 + D2)                closed   → AC #2
  ddx-98e6e9ef  routing-preflight-gate (D3)                   closed   → AC #3
  ddx-dd4c423d  routing-drain-regression test                 closed   → AC #10

Code evidence the closed work is in tree:
  cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go:20-21
    test seams verify ResolveProfileLadder/ResolveTierModelRef are NOT called
    on the default path (per ddx-755f5881 AC #3)
  cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:84-120
    DispatchWorker normalizes empty input to {Profile: "default"} only
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:360-392
    routing preflight gate runs BEFORE Claim at line 394 (D3, ddx-98e6e9ef)
  cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1683-1684
    --escalate / --override-model flags exist; tier ladder + model_overrides
    are off by default (consistent with D2/D4 design)
  cli/internal/config/types.go:230-234
    migration note: agent.routing.default_harness was REMOVED in ddx-87fb72c2

================================================================================
What is open (blocks tracker close)
================================================================================

ddx-87fb72c2  routing-config-deprecation                      open  → AC #4
              hard error on default_harness; ladders/overrides opt-in only;
              CHANGELOG migration entry. (Note: the field-removal half appears
              landed per types.go:230-234 — verify what residual scope this
              bead still owns before executing it.)

ddx-2589e256  routing-overrides-e2e                           open  → AC #5
              --harness/--model/--profile flag plumbing parity tests for
              ResolveRoute(Profile, Harness, Model) shape; cost-tiebreak e2e;
              typed-error surfacing for incompatible combos.

ddx-950321ef  routing-frontend-goldens                        open  → AC #6
              Playwright drain-queue fixtures matching the real failing
              config; Drain button → live-endpoint-backed harness;
              never gemini+non-gemini-model; never 19-burn.

ddx-653f6ac9  routing-cleanup                                 open  → AC #7
              dead-code detector for orphaned compensating logic;
              remove residual allow-list / exact-pin / cost-scoring code
              that re-implements upstream behavior.

ddx-a0cf398b  routing-followups                               open  → AC #8
              file the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer
              bead; close out D6/D7 housekeeping.

================================================================================
Why this bead does not get implementation in this pass
================================================================================

1. The epic's AC is the union of its children's AC. Every remaining AC item
   (#4, #5, #6, #7, #8, plus the documentation half of #9) is the contract of
   a specific open child bead. Implementing them here would either duplicate
   work that the child bead will redo, or steal scope that another worker
   may already be planning to claim.

2. The bead description's "Sequencing" section explicitly orders:
     1. upstream lands (done)
     2. go.mod bump (done — ddx-967e9802)
     3. D1+D2+D3+D5 land together (D1+D2=ddx-755f5881 done; D3=ddx-98e6e9ef
        done; D5=ddx-2589e256 OPEN)
     4. D4 (ddx-87fb72c2) lands with or right after the above (OPEN)
     5. follow-ups (ddx-a0cf398b, ddx-950321ef, ddx-653f6ac9) (OPEN)
   The tracker is meant to close once steps 3–5 complete via their child
   beads, not to carry independent code.

3. Attempting any of {D4, override e2e, playwright goldens, dead-code sweep}
   in this single execute-bead pass would land a sprawling cross-cutting
   commit that violates the per-child-bead acceptance contracts and is
   hard to review. Per CLAUDE.md "DOWITYTD" and the merge-policy memory,
   the right action is to execute the children individually.

================================================================================
What a follow-up attempt should do
================================================================================

This tracker should NOT be re-queued for direct execution. Instead:

  a) Execute the four open child beads in their natural order:
        ddx-87fb72c2 (D4 — config deprecation residue + CHANGELOG)
        ddx-2589e256 (D5 — override flag e2e)
        ddx-950321ef (D6-equivalent — Playwright goldens)
        ddx-653f6ac9 (cleanup / dead-code sweep)
        ddx-a0cf398b (file follow-up beads)
     Each closes one or two AC items on this tracker.

  b) When all five close, re-open this tracker briefly to:
        - verify every AC item maps to a closed child or in-tree evidence
        - update DDx CHANGELOG (AC #9) if not already done by ddx-967e9802
        - close ddx-fdd3ea36 with a closing-comment that points at each
          child bead's closing commit.

That close step is documentation/bookkeeping — a single small commit, well
suited to a future execute-bead pass once the children are green.

No code changes were made in this pass. Workspace is clean.

created_at:2026-05-03T11:09:20.805718788Z kind:cost source:ddx agent execute-bead summary:tokens=8503 cost_usd=0.8610] map[actor:erik body:Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits. ## Summary The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector. These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either: (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned. The bead's "SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make. ## Evidence 1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims). 2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: "Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands." Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`. 3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites). 4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM ("ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec. 5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands. ## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict) The bead also fails the size check on its own: - 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run). - Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals. - File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably >500 LOC across >5 files in unrelated packages. - Per execution-bead protocol size check: "More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems" + "More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages" → DECOMPOSE. ## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt The protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human: Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct. Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one. Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded. Each option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick. ## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage 1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task. 2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead "add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway). 3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate. 4. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped. ## What this attempt did NOT do (and why) - No code changes. The conflict is upstream of any implementation choice. - No child beads filed. Filing children would commit to the unification path; see "Why I am not decomposing" above. - No partial fix to the 5 dropped flags. The bead explicitly absorbs that narrow fix; doing it here without addressing the unification framing would muddy the bead's status. created_at:2026-05-03T11:09:21.004532662Z kind:no_changes_needs_investigation source:ddx agent execute-loop summary:no_changes_needs_investigation] map[actor:erik body:no_changes

Scope:
- In cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go, after the VerdictBlock and VerdictRequestChanges branches that already record events and reopen, call into triage.Decide(beadID, mode=review_block, history) to choose the next action.
- Track the per-bead BLOCK count by reading prior 'review' events with Summary='BLOCK' from the bead store. No new persistent state needed.
- Implement the three actions for review_block:
  - re_attempt_with_context: leave bead reopened (current behavior) so next drain pass picks it up; ensure the prompt assembler picks up the BLOCK rationale (already in bead notes via Reopen()).
  - escalate_tier: set a metadata hint or tier-pin so the next attempt runs at a higher tier (use existing escalation.ModelTier mechanism).
  - needs_human: add 'needs_human' label to the bead and emit a triage-decision event.
- Surface review-pairing-degraded events in the triage decision (AC#6 of parent): when the latest BLOCK was paired with a degraded reviewer, prefer re_attempt_with_context with a fresh reviewer pair rather than escalating.

The work described by this bead's AC was implemented and merged in commit
e188d9d3 ("feat(agent): wire triage dispatch for
lock_contention/execution_failed/no_changes [...]"), which is an ancestor of
the current execute-bead base 26d80c83. That commit added 755 lines across
three files:

  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go    (+3)
  cli/internal/agent/triage_dispatch.go        (+405)
  cli/internal/agent/triage_dispatch_test.go   (+347)

AC mapping (all satisfied by code already present in the worktree):

  AC#1 — Lock-contention classification + retry-with-backoff with ≥3 steps
    before escalation:
    - IsLockContentionError (triage_dispatch.go) matches .git/index.lock,
      "another git process seems to be running", staging-tracker / tracker
      lock messages.
    - ClassifyAttemptForTriage routes execution_failed reports whose Detail
      matches IsLockContentionError to triage.FailureModeLockContention.
    - RunWithLockBackoff retries an AttemptFunc against a backoff schedule;
      LockContentionBackoff defaults to {30s, 90s, 300s} (three slots),
      sets Exhausted=true after consuming all slots so callers escalate.
    - DefaultPolicy.Ladders[FailureModeLockContention] =
      {retry_with_backoff, retry_with_backoff, needs_human} so cross-pass
      lock_contention also escalates.

  AC#2 — execution_failed enters triage and produces escalate_tier or
    needs_human depending on attempt count:
    - DefaultPolicy.Ladders[FailureModeExecutionFailed] =
      {re_attempt_with_context, escalate_tier, needs_human}.
    - DispatchPostAttempt records a triage-decision event per attempt;
      HistoryFromEvents recovers the prior-mode count so the ladder
      advances per occurrence (verified by
      TestDispatchExecutionFailedEscalates).

  AC#3 — no_changes with rationale not citing AC items files a clarification
    follow-up and closes the original:
    - RationaleCitesAC returns false on vague rationales and true when the
      rationale (a) is "specific" via rationaleIsSpecific (cites SHA, test
      name, file path) or (b) shares ≥2 AC content tokens of length ≥6.
    - DispatchPostAttempt, on ActionFileFollowup for FailureModeNoChanges,
      calls fileNoChangesFollowup which creates a child bead inheriting
      labels/spec-id, adds kind:clarification, embeds the worker's
      rationale and parent AC, and back-links via the
      triage_followup_for / triage_followup_reason Extra fields. Then
      CloseWithEvidence closes the parent (BaseRev as closing SHA).

  AC#4 — Tests cover all four scenarios:
    - TestRunWithLockBackoffSucceedsOnRetry — lock-error retry success
    - TestRunWithLockBackoffExhausts — backoff exhaustion across all three
      schedule slots
    - TestDispatchExecutionFailedEscalates — full execution_failed ladder
      (re_attempt → escalate_tier → needs_human, then clamps)
    - TestDispatchNoChangesFilesFollowup — weak-rationale no_changes files
      a clarification child and closes the parent
    Plus TestIsLockContentionError, TestClassifyAttemptForTriage,
    TestRationaleCitesAC, TestRunWithLockBackoffPropagatesNonLockError,
    TestRunWithLockBackoffRetriesOnAttemptError,
    TestDispatchNoChangesStrongRationaleSkipsTriage as supporting cases.

Verification:
  cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/ -run \
    'TestRunWithLockBackoff|TestDispatch|TestClassifyAttemptForTriage|TestIsLockContentionError|TestRationaleCitesAC' \
    -count=1
  → ok  github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent  0.024s

rationale: status: already_complete

The bead's implementation was committed as 83ec7853 ("feat(server): reportedWorkers GraphQL query exposes worker_ingest derived view") in a prior execution attempt and merged into main via 185ab68e. The current base-rev (5fe31689) already contains the full implementation:

  - cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql           (reportedWorkers query + ReportedWorker/WorkerFreshness types)
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_reported_workers.go  (resolver reading s.workerIngest snapshot)
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/generated.go             (regenerated gqlgen)
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go                (ReportedWorker + WorkerFreshness models)
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver.go              (resolver wiring)
  - cli/internal/server/reported_workers_adapter.go      (snapshot -> model adapter using freshnessState)
  - cli/internal/server/worker_ingest.go                 (snapshot accessor)
  - cli/internal/server/server.go                        (workerIngest registry handle)
  - cli/internal/server/graphql_reported_workers_test.go (TestGraphQL_Workers_FreshnessFields with connected/stale/disconnected sub-tests + TestGraphQL_Workers_MultipleWorkersPerProject)

The earlier REVIEW:BLOCK note was raised against an attempt whose diff contained only execution-evidence files; the actual feature commit (83ec7853) had already been merged ahead of that review and the BLOCK is stale. All AC items are satisfied in HEAD and the targeted tests pass.

Child process PID is recorded on WorkerRecord; watchdog SIGTERM/SIGKILL via PID still functions (existing test scenarios pass with subprocess-stub). 3. Progress events from child reach the parent via /api/workers/{id}/event (or the per-worker event sink), and SSE subscribers still see them (graphql_subscription_worker_test.go assertions hold). 4. Stop() cancels ctx AND signals the child PID; no orphans; existing workers_stop*_test.go assertions hold against subprocess-stub. 5. workers_test.go, workers_costcap_test.go, workers_gates_test.go, workers_watchdog_test.go, workers_wake_test.go, workers_reap_unix.go, workers_stop*_test.go, workers_e2e_test.go updated to use subprocess-stub instead of BeadWorkerFactory injection. 6. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker untouched — still uses ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec (deletion deferred to 7b). 7. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-03T06:54:27.558467012Z","description":"Replace in-process runWorker (cli/internal/server/workers.go) with subprocess exec of 'ddx work'. The existing /api/workers/register + /api/workers/{id}/event endpoints (ADR-022 step 1) become the parent server's window into the child process. Keep ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct in place for now — handler still calls StartExecuteLoop(spec); spec is translated to argv/env at the exec call site. Watchdog SIGTERM/SIGKILL works via the existing PID field on WorkerRecord. Drop in-process land-coordinator coupling, in-process post-merge reviewer, in-process cost-cap, in-process route-preflight from this code path — all of those now live inside the child 'ddx work' process. Update workers_test.go and related package tests that injected BeadWorkerFactory: replace in-process injection with subprocess-stub (a tiny shell or built-in test binary that simulates ddx work's exec contract). Lifecycle/Stop/wake/reap behaviours preserved by reading the child's worker-events.jsonl + signalling the PID.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"detail":"routing preflight rejected (harness=codex model=): no viable routing candidate for pins harness=codex: 1 candidates rejected","harness":"codex","model":"","reason":"preflight_rejected"}","created_at":"2026-05-03T06:54:51.93719862Z","kind":"disruption_detected","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"preflight_rejected"}],"id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T065047-61e4ed20-c4de2d6e","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:server","kind:refactor","adr:022"],"parent":"ddx-eccc6efb","priority":2,"status":"closed","title":"ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker → exec ddx work subprocess (progress via /api/workers/{id}/event, watchdog via PID)","updated_at":"2026-05-03T06:55:13.55742866Z"}
 {"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/server/workers.go:runWorker exec's the resolved ddx binary (os.Executable() with DDX_EXECUTABLE override) with subcommand 'work' and a flag-vector derived from ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec. No in-process call to worker.Run remains in this code path.
2. WorkerRecord.PID is populated with the subprocess PID before the exec returns to StartExecuteLoop; signal-based Stop and watchdog reaping continue to work against the child PID (TestStopExecuteLoopWorker_KillsSubprocess covers SIGTERM→SIGKILL grace).
3. Progress events from the child reach the parent's drainProgress goroutine via the existing /api/workers/<id>/event ingestion path (server.go:884). At least one new test TestRunWorker_ProgressBridge_FromChild asserts that an event POSTed by the child appears on a subscribed SSE channel.
4. BeadWorkerFactory is deleted from WorkerManager. workers_test.go/workers_e2e_test.go/workers_stop_propagation_test.go/workers_route_preflight_test.go/workers_config_test.go/graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go updated: tests that depended on injecting an in-process executor are rewritten using the test-reentry pattern (ddx-work-stub) or deleted with rationale comment if the semantics moved into cli/cmd/* tests.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-03T22:59:39.685160971Z","description":"PROBLEM: cli/internal/server/workers.go:runWorker runs the bead-execution loop in-process, knitting together LandCoordinator, DefaultBeadReviewer, CostCapTracker, RoutePreflight, ExecuteBeadWorker, ProgressCh→drainProgress, WakeCh, BeadWorkerFactory injection, and watchdog phase-stall tracking — all of which contradict ADR-022's autonomous-default model where workers are subprocesses identical to operator-launched 'ddx work'.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-940 (runWorker) builds an in-process executor closure (singleTierAttempt at lines 679-747), wraps it with a CostCap tracker (lines 753-797), wires a DefaultBeadReviewer (lines 801-809), and calls worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{...}). All of this duplicates work that 'ddx work' (cli/cmd/work.go → cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:runAgentExecuteLoopImpl) already does, just inside the parent server process.

PROPOSED FIX: Replace the in-process worker.Run(...) call with exec.CommandContext(ctx, dddxBin, 'work', <flags>) where dddxBin = os.Executable() (override-able via DDX_EXECUTABLE env for tests). Project root passed via --project flag or DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env. Provider/harness/model/profile/effort/poll-interval/once/no-review/review-harness/review-model/label-filter/model-ref translated to existing ddx work flags. Capture child PID into WorkerRecord.PID (already a field at workers.go:147). Stop() and the watchdog already signal via PID (workers.go:1070, 1279) — verify the cross-process path works; SIGTERM with kill grace then SIGKILL.

Progress event ingestion changes: child process emits via workerprobe to /api/workers/<id>/event (this endpoint exists, server.go:884). The parent's WorkerManager subscribes to those events (in-memory derived view from the workers ingestion handler) and forwards into the per-subprocess workerHandle.progressCh so existing SSE subscribers and drainProgress logic continue to work. Mapping: parent records the WorkerRecord.id at exec time; child registers with /api/workers/register and gets a probe-issued worker_id; parent correlates by ProjectRoot + ExecutorPID + StartedAt window.

Drop in-process LandCoordinator/Reviewer/CostCap/RoutePreflight wiring inside runWorker (they now run inside the child). LandCoordinators registry stays for the existing inline 'ddx work' path (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1714).

WakeCh: replace with SIGUSR1 to the child (or document as no-op for the v1 of this refactor; WakeProject still walks workers but only sends to live in-process handles — kept null-safe for subprocess workers).

BeadWorkerFactory: removed. Tests that previously injected a fake executor migrate to either (a) reentry-stub mode where the test binary acts as 'ddx work' via go test re-exec pattern, or (b) deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now live in cli/cmd/* tests).

NON-SCOPE: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker rewrite (covered by 7b). Deletion of ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct (covered by 7b). End-to-end real-claude integration (covered by 7c). UI/GraphQL surface changes (already covered by ADR-022 step 5).","id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T225413-91120fa0-5be5f460","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:server","kind:refactor","adr:022"],"parent":"ddx-eccc6efb","priority":2,"status":"closed","title":"ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker exec's ddx work subprocess (parent-side plumbing)","updated_at":"2026-05-03T23:00:18.503933642Z"}

LAYER 2 refactors the underlying resolver so the existing /graphql endpoint can also safely serve multiple projects.

PROBLEM
The GraphQL resolver is constructed once with WorkingDir as a struct field (cli/internal/server/server.go:4701 → cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_documents.go:26 etc.). The field is immutable per request. LAYER 1 works around this by reconstructing the resolver per scoped request, but the original /graphql route still has the structural issue.

LAYER 2 SCOPE
Refactor the GraphQL resolver to take WorkingDir from REQUEST CONTEXT, not a struct field. Thread project context through all resolvers via Go context.Context.

This makes both routes (/graphql and /api/projects/{project}/graphql) project-aware uniformly. The scoped middleware injects the WorkingDir; the unscoped trusted route falls back to s.WorkingDir.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Adding new GraphQL queries (out of scope)
- Reshaping the schema
- LAYER 3 (Bead/Execution Node(id) resolution)

Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>].","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:41.651384066Z","description":"The ddx try / ddx work execution machinery has 9 fail-prone layers (routing pre-flight, cooldown classification, claim CAS, worktree spawn, subprocess lifecycle, outcome adjudication, retry policy, land coordination, review). Today most layers fail-CLOSED — when a layer's specific check rejects, the layer wedges the entire pipeline. The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours.

The 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance.

PRINCIPLE STATEMENTS

P1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.

P2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell.

P3: OBSERVABLE DEGRADATION. Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see "preflight skipped (no operator pin)" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.

P4: BOUNDED BLAST RADIUS. A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. Stay-alive fix at commit 41cb762e established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend to all layers.

P5: OPERATOR-VISIBLE STATE. Worker reports current state (idle, claiming, executing, reviewing, blocked-on-X) at all times. No '8 hours running, 0 attempts' mystery state. ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model defines the worker side; UI workers panel surfaces it.

P6: AUTO-RETRY ONLY FOR TRANSIENT CLASSES. Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race → no cooldown, return to ready. Existing code: shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1545 already respects Disrupted (commit 47d8054e).

P7: BEAD = PROMPT. A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked. If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Code changes (this bead is doc-only)
- Per-layer reliability bead-quality fixes (separate refactor children)

INTERSECTIONS
- Each refactor child (ddx-c8f79963 C5, ddx-06eb05d8 C7, ddx-9228a484 C9, ddx-848069a3 C8, ddx-b669bb9f C6, ddx-c670ef0a C12) gets an AC line citing the principle(s) it enforces
- Bead-quality audit applies P7 to ~20 existing open beads","id":"ddx-06b77652","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","area:server","kind:design","reliability","bead-quality"],"parent":"ddx-e34994e2","priority":2,"status":"open","title":"ADR-style: 7 reliability principles for ddx try / ddx work / sub-agent execution","updated_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:41.651384066Z"}

  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.
  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.
  - Add TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips and TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce to a new cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Any change to SetExecutionCooldown duration policy (controlled by caller constants).","id":"ddx-06eb05d8","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","refactor","kind:refactor"],"parent":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","priority":1,"status":"open","title":"C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps + pick() derives from drain.result","updated_at":"2026-05-03T20:09:24.677741406Z"}

Reviewed and approved by user before any implementation beads are filed from the roadmap.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:14:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"4025192","closing_commit_sha":"fabf513096b522e921020e323ef1bf4a9cf36afa","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:43:27.404796076Z","description":"Operator-observed gap: when ddx-server restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the orchestrator. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).

PROPOSED ARCHITECTURAL SHIFT
WORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API. --local mode collapses to "worker registers with itself" using an in-process implementation of the same API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

THE ADR EVALUATES AND CHOOSES AMONG

PROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients
- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)
- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)
- Worker polls /api/workers/<id>/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server
- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/<id>/event {kind, body}
- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/<id>/result {bead-id, status, evidence}
- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters "disconnected" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return
- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead

PROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous
- Worker runs independently; no server required
- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort
- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator
- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)
- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel

PROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present
- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup
- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue
- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store
- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)

UNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)

POST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }
POST /api/workers/<id>/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }
GET  /api/workers/<id>/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }
POST /api/workers/<id>/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/disconnect → 204 (graceful)

The existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

OUTSTANDING BEADS THIS ADR MUST RECONCILE (overlap analysis required in the ADR output)

ABSORBED (implementation subsumed by the new architecture; ADR explicitly closes these as superseded once roadmap lands):
- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): becomes the API request/response payload — the spec IS the wire format. Refactor concern dissolves.
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): ALREADY FIXED at commit 41cb762e via flat 30s poll-interval default + attempted-map reset. Whether the new architecture preserves these tactical fixes or replaces them via API-driven semantics needs an ADR decision.
- ddx-cfedee8e (escalation: wire ladder): the ladder becomes server-side orchestration logic, not loop-body logic. ADR must specify where ladder lives in the new shape.

CONFLICTS WITH IN-FLIGHT REFACTOR (refactor epic ddx-5cb6e6cd children) — ADR must resolve sequencing:
- ddx-c8f79963 (C5: no_changes adjudication move into try.Attempt): refactors the SAME code that the API transition refactors. Either land C5 first then transition, OR fold C5 into the transition.
- ddx-06eb05d8 (C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps): same surface; same choice.
- ddx-848069a3 (C8: routing preflight to Drain startup): related to "server-side preflight" decision in API design.
- ddx-9228a484 (C9: StopCondition + cost-cap, absorbs ddx-cfedee8e and ddx-dc157075): heavy overlap. ADR must decide whether StopCondition becomes a server-side concept, a worker-side concept, or both.
- ddx-387a0178 (C13: file/identifier rename + ddx try CLI command): the ADR's new package layout (cli/internal/agent/work/) may conflict with C13's rename plan. Coordinate.
- ddx-1d867ec1 (rename execute_bead_loop.go): same as C13.

PARALLEL-SAFE (no conflict; ADR notes them but doesn't reshape):
- ddx-5b3e57f4 (cooldown: distinguish disrupted from gave-up): the API contract makes disruption naturally observable (server sees worker disconnect → marks Disrupted). But the cooldown logic itself is independent. ADR should call out that the API design enables cleaner Disrupted classification.
- ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute upstream gap): independent of API design.
- ddx-4c51d33e LAYER 1, ddx-055e8d32 LAYER 2, ddx-5ae050dc LAYER 3 (GraphQL project scoping): all in cli/internal/server/graphql/. Independent of worker API.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker bug): already fixed.
- ddx-a946c744 + ddx-aee651ec + ddx-3e60fd84 + ddx-83440482 (REACH/checks): hook into pre_merge gate; needs to integrate with the new worker contract but doesn't block ADR.
- All Story 6/14/15/16 frontend + backend feature beads: independent.

SPEC UPDATES THIS ADR DRIVES (the ADR roadmap must include these as deliverables)

- New ADR document: docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- FEAT-006 (Agent Service) — major amendment: worker API contract section, --local-collapses-to-API explanation, deprecation of direct execution paths
- FEAT-010 (Executions) — amendment: worker lifecycle now spans across server restarts; "execution" becomes a bead-attempt regardless of which worker performs it
- SD-024 (Resolved Config / CLIOverrides) — possible amendment: if ResolvedConfig becomes part of the API request payload, SD-024 reshapes
- TD-030 (Backend interface + chaos contract) — cross-reference if any
- CONTRACT-003 (Fizeau Service) — no change required (fizeau is below the worker; the API is between worker and ddx-server, not worker and fizeau)

NOT IN SCOPE FOR THIS ADR
- Specific code changes (this TD documents the design; implementation is follow-up beads from the roadmap)
- Multi-server federation (each server owns its workers; cross-server is a separate concern)
- Auth beyond session tokens (the worker-server channel is local; no remote workers in v1)
- Reshaping the bead schema (out of scope; ADR-004 owns that)

OUTPUTS (TD deliverables)
- ADR document at docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- Updated FEAT-006 (or proposed amendments listed)
- Sequence diagrams for: register, claim, execute, restart-recovery, graceful-shutdown
- Compatibility table: which existing tests/behaviors break, which migrate cleanly
- Implementation roadmap: ordered list of beads to file as follow-ups
- Overlap reconciliation table: each overlapping bead (above) classified as ABSORBED / CONFLICT-RESOLVED / PARALLEL-SAFE with explicit rationale

SEQUENCING DIRECTIVE (per user direction, 2026-05-03)

Reviewed and approved by user before any implementation beads are filed from the roadmap.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:14:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"4025192","closing_commit_sha":"fabf513096b522e921020e323ef1bf4a9cf36afa","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:43:27.404796076Z","description":"Operator-observed gap: when ddx-server restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the orchestrator. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).

PROPOSED ARCHITECTURAL SHIFT
WORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API. --local mode collapses to "worker registers with itself" using an in-process implementation of the same API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

THE ADR EVALUATES AND CHOOSES AMONG

PROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients
- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)
- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)
- Worker polls /api/workers/<id>/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server
- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/<id>/event {kind, body}
- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/<id>/result {bead-id, status, evidence}
- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters "disconnected" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return
- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead

PROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous
- Worker runs independently; no server required
- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort
- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator
- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)
- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel

PROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present
- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup
- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue
- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store
- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)

UNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)

POST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }
POST /api/workers/<id>/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }
GET  /api/workers/<id>/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }
POST /api/workers/<id>/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/disconnect → 204 (graceful)

The existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

OUTSTANDING BEADS THIS ADR MUST RECONCILE (overlap analysis required in the ADR output)

ABSORBED (implementation subsumed by the new architecture; ADR explicitly closes these as superseded once roadmap lands):
- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): becomes the API request/response payload — the spec IS the wire format. Refactor concern dissolves.
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): ALREADY FIXED at commit 41cb762e via flat 30s poll-interval default + attempted-map reset. Whether the new architecture preserves these tactical fixes or replaces them via API-driven semantics needs an ADR decision.
- ddx-cfedee8e (escalation: wire ladder): the ladder becomes server-side orchestration logic, not loop-body logic. ADR must specify where ladder lives in the new shape.

CONFLICTS WITH IN-FLIGHT REFACTOR (refactor epic ddx-5cb6e6cd children) — ADR must resolve sequencing:
- ddx-c8f79963 (C5: no_changes adjudication move into try.Attempt): refactors the SAME code that the API transition refactors. Either land C5 first then transition, OR fold C5 into the transition.
- ddx-06eb05d8 (C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps): same surface; same choice.
- ddx-848069a3 (C8: routing preflight to Drain startup): related to "server-side preflight" decision in API design.
- ddx-9228a484 (C9: StopCondition + cost-cap, absorbs ddx-cfedee8e and ddx-dc157075): heavy overlap. ADR must decide whether StopCondition becomes a server-side concept, a worker-side concept, or both.
- ddx-387a0178 (C13: file/identifier rename + ddx try CLI command): the ADR's new package layout (cli/internal/agent/work/) may conflict with C13's rename plan. Coordinate.
- ddx-1d867ec1 (rename execute_bead_loop.go): same as C13.

PARALLEL-SAFE (no conflict; ADR notes them but doesn't reshape):
- ddx-5b3e57f4 (cooldown: distinguish disrupted from gave-up): the API contract makes disruption naturally observable (server sees worker disconnect → marks Disrupted). But the cooldown logic itself is independent. ADR should call out that the API design enables cleaner Disrupted classification.
- ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute upstream gap): independent of API design.
- ddx-4c51d33e LAYER 1, ddx-055e8d32 LAYER 2, ddx-5ae050dc LAYER 3 (GraphQL project scoping): all in cli/internal/server/graphql/. Independent of worker API.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker bug): already fixed.
- ddx-a946c744 + ddx-aee651ec + ddx-3e60fd84 + ddx-83440482 (REACH/checks): hook into pre_merge gate; needs to integrate with the new worker contract but doesn't block ADR.
- All Story 6/14/15/16 frontend + backend feature beads: independent.

SPEC UPDATES THIS ADR DRIVES (the ADR roadmap must include these as deliverables)

- New ADR document: docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- FEAT-006 (Agent Service) — major amendment: worker API contract section, --local-collapses-to-API explanation, deprecation of direct execution paths
- FEAT-010 (Executions) — amendment: worker lifecycle now spans across server restarts; "execution" becomes a bead-attempt regardless of which worker performs it
- SD-024 (Resolved Config / CLIOverrides) — possible amendment: if ResolvedConfig becomes part of the API request payload, SD-024 reshapes
- TD-030 (Backend interface + chaos contract) — cross-reference if any
- CONTRACT-003 (Fizeau Service) — no change required (fizeau is below the worker; the API is between worker and ddx-server, not worker and fizeau)

NOT IN SCOPE FOR THIS ADR
- Specific code changes (this TD documents the design; implementation is follow-up beads from the roadmap)
- Multi-server federation (each server owns its workers; cross-server is a separate concern)
- Auth beyond session tokens (the worker-server channel is local; no remote workers in v1)
- Reshaping the bead schema (out of scope; ADR-004 owns that)

OUTPUTS (TD deliverables)
- ADR document at docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- Updated FEAT-006 (or proposed amendments listed)
- Sequence diagrams for: register, claim, execute, restart-recovery, graceful-shutdown
- Compatibility table: which existing tests/behaviors break, which migrate cleanly
- Implementation roadmap: ordered list of beads to file as follow-ups
- Overlap reconciliation table: each overlapping bead (above) classified as ABSORBED / CONFLICT-RESOLVED / PARALLEL-SAFE with explicit rationale

SEQUENCING DIRECTIVE (per user direction, 2026-05-03)
- Should land BEFORE major refactor children that touch the loop body (C5, C7, C9) — otherwise they'd refactor against the OLD model and need re-doing
- Should NOT block routine bug fixes or feature beads
- C5/C7/C9 explicitly FROZEN until the ADR's roadmap establishes the new package layout","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","fallback_chain":[],"requested_harness":"claude"}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:18:38.009514865Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260503T021424-dac100b6","harness":"claude","input_tokens":21,"output_tokens":14799,"total_tokens":14820,"cost_usd":1.0767455,"duration_ms":249728,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:18:38.0358447Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=14820 cost_usd=1.0767"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"","resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:18:43.263235583Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"erik","body":"APPROVE

8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-02T07:04:50.205555594Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-0793ac75","depends_on_id":"ddx-fe63969f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T07:04:50Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-0793ac75","depends_on_id":"ddx-387a0178","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:53Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
  Story 14 requires federated query resolvers that aggregate beads, runs, and projects across registered spokes. Today the GraphQL schema at cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql defines FederatedBead, FederatedRun, and FederatedProject types and the corresponding query fields, but write-routing (hub forwards mutations to owning spoke) is not implemented, and the resolver_federation.go file has no mutation forwarding.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go:61 defines FederatedBeads, :124 defines FederatedRuns, and :184 defines FederatedProjects — all read-only fan-out via FederationProvider.FanOut (cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go:31). cli/internal/federation/fanout.go:208 provides the FanOutClient.Execute fan-out transport. No mutation resolver in resolver_mutation_*.go calls FanOut or routes writes to an owning spoke; mutations remain hub-local. The ownerSpoke routing metadata fields (nodeID, spokeURL) are populated by existing fan-out code but no write-routing contract enforces "hub forwards, never broadcasts."

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add write-routing helper in cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go: routeToOwner(ctx, ownerSpokeURL, mutationQuery, variables) error that forwards mutations via HTTP to the owning spoke.
  - Wire routeToOwner into the relevant mutation resolvers in resolver_mutation_beads.go or resolver_mutation_runs.go for any mutation that operates on a federated resource.
  - Add schema.graphql comment block documenting the write-routing contract ("hub forwards to ownerSpoke; never broadcasts").
  - Name the ownerSpoke field on FederatedBead/FederatedRun/FederatedProject as the routing discriminator.

NON-SCOPE
  - New non-federated resolvers (covered by Story 13).
  - Spoke discovery and registration (covered by ddx-fe63969f).
  - Federated mutations on FederatedProject (out of Story 14 scope; read-only for projects).","id":"ddx-0793ac75","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","story:14","area:server","kind:feature"],"parent":"ddx-a038a090","priority":2,"status":"open","title":"federation: federated GraphQL resolvers + routing-metadata fields","updated_at":"2026-05-03T20:10:24.948417527Z"}

 {"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat.go exports withHeartbeat(ctx, beadID, interval, store, fn) with the signature defined above.
2. execute_bead_loop.go:732-753 replaced with a single withHeartbeat call; no inline goroutine + WaitGroup remains in the loop body.
3. Drain loop caller of withHeartbeat is the production site (execute_bead_loop.go or its successor after C13 rename).
4. TestWithHeartbeat_TickerFiredBeforeReturn in cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat_test.go: injects a stub ticker; verifies Heartbeat called at least once before fn returns.
5. TestWithHeartbeat_CancelPropagates: cancels ctx before fn returns; verifies goroutine exits without blocking.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/agent/work/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28.569636417Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-0b8780db","depends_on_id":"ddx-06eb05d8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
  The heartbeat goroutine in the drain loop is inlined, coupling ticker lifecycle management with candidate execution and blocking unit tests from verifying heartbeat behavior without a real ticker.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:732-753 inlines a goroutine + sync.WaitGroup + time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval) directly in the loop body. hbCtx and hbWG are declared locally; the goroutine calls w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID) on every tick. There is no injection seam, so tests cannot stub the ticker to verify heartbeat cadence or cancellation without real wall-clock delays.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Extract to withHeartbeat(ctx context.Context, beadID string, interval time.Duration, store interface{ Heartbeat(string) error }, fn func() (ExecuteBeadReport, error)) error in a new file cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat.go.
  - withHeartbeat starts the internal goroutine + WaitGroup, calls fn(), cancels the ticker goroutine on fn return, and returns fn's result.
  - Drain loop body (execute_bead_loop.go:732) replaces the inline goroutine block with a single withHeartbeat call.
  - Add TestWithHeartbeat_TickerFiredBeforeReturn and TestWithHeartbeat_CancelPropagates to cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat_test.go using a stub ticker channel injection.

NON-SCOPE
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Changes to heartbeatInterval policy or the Store.Heartbeat call itself.
  - Any behavior change to the drain loop; this is a pure extraction.","id":"ddx-0b8780db","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","refactor","kind:refactor"],"parent":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","priority":2,"status":"open","title":"C11: heartbeat extracted to withHeartbeat helper","updated_at":"2026-05-03T20:09:40.552661339Z"}

Decisions to make during implementation (carry over from this conversation):

- Whether closed-bead events arrays move into attachment storage (.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl) or stay inline in beads-archive.
- Migration semantics for the existing beads.jsonl (split in place vs explicit migrate command).

 {"acceptance":"1. cli/tools/lint/routinglint/ (package routinglint) contains the new rule or an integration test that enforces the Profile/Harness/Model-on-Runtime prohibition for run/try/work paths.
2. TestRoutingLint_RunPathNeverSetsProfileDirectly in cli/tools/lint/routinglint/ (or a new integration test file): runs go run ./tools/lint/routinglint/cmd/routinglint ./... against the live tree and asserts zero violations for the run/try/work pattern.
3. TestViolations (cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer_test.go:12) still passes.
4. TestClean (cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer_test.go:21) still passes.
5. cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1891-1897 CLIOverrides usage verified compliant (CLIOverrides is not a Runtime struct; no lint exemption needed).
6. Any actual violations found in run/work/try paths are fixed or annotated with routinglint:legacy-rejection reason="...".
7. cd cli && go test ./tools/lint/routinglint/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-02T07:02:50.959795835Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-0ca4ffda","depends_on_id":"ddx-cfedee8e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T07:02:50Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
  The run/try/work dispatch paths must not set Profile, Harness, or Model directly on ResolvedConfig or Runtime structs — those are durable knobs that belong in config.LoadAndResolve. Without a mechanically-enforced lint rule, future edits can silently reintroduce the anti-pattern and bypass the fizeau routing contract.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go (package routinglint) currently forbids retired compensating-routing identifiers (ResolveProfileLadder, ResolveTierModelRef, etc.) and flag literals (--escalate, profile_ladders). It does not yet forbid direct field assignment of Profile/Harness/Model on Runtime structs inside run/try/work command code paths. The SD-024 runtimelint analyzer (cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/analyzer.go:41) forbids durable-knob fields on *Runtime structs generically, but does not name a per-command allowlist or produce a test that verifies the run/work/try paths specifically. No TestRoutingLint* test exercises this invariant.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Extend routinglint (cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go) to flag direct Profile/Harness/Model field assignment on types matching *Runtime in cli/cmd/ run/work/try command files, or add a new rule in runtimelint covering this.
  - Alternatively, add a targeted integration test TestRoutingLint_RunPathNeverSetsProfileDirectly that runs the analyzer against the live tree and asserts zero diagnostics for the assignment pattern.

Adding cli/internal/agent/executeloop creates a new production entry package that the reachability checker must understand, or later REACH-PROTO work can incorrectly report the new spec code as unreachable.

ROOT CAUSE
The parent bead explicitly notes REACH-PROTO ddx-a946c744 must know about the new cli/internal/agent/executeloop package as an entry root. Without a tracker slice, the implementation children can land a new package before the reachability rules are updated.

PROPOSED FIX
- Locate the production-reachability checker configuration or entry-root list introduced by ddx-a946c744.
- Add cli/internal/agent/executeloop as an allowed production entry root once the canonical package exists.
- Add or update the checker test that proves the package is reachable/accepted.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not modify ExecuteLoopSpec behavior.
- Do not change unrelated reachability rules.

OUTCOME: no source changes. All four AC are satisfied by code already merged on main
at base-rev a9726cd3 (this worktree's base). The prior BLOCK was on an attempt whose
diff only contained execution evidence; this rationale points at the evidence in the
already-landed commits so a re-review can resolve the BLOCK.

Primary commit:
  8d1edfa4 fix(web): drop stale artifacts loadMore responses on search race [ddx-18b15451]
  Files:
    cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.svelte
    cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/searchFetcher.ts
    cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/searchFetcher.test.ts

Supporting prior commit (added the `search:` arg to the GraphQL query and wired it
into the SSR load() so search is server-side from page 1, not just past page 1):
  13896420 fix(artifacts): mediaType wildcard + server-side search + sort/staleness args [ddx-0ac5f35d]

AC mapping (all satisfied):

1. Frontend search calls server.
   - +page.ts:7-27 — ARTIFACTS_QUERY declares `$search: String` and forwards it to
     `artifacts(search: $search, ...)`.
   - +page.ts:84-117 — load() reads `q` from the URL and passes it as `search` to the
     server on initial render and on every navigation.
   - +page.svelte:67-78,87 — onSearchInput debounces then calls navigateWith({ q }),
     which goto()s the new URL, re-running load() against the server.
   - +page.svelte:168-192 — loadMore() also passes `search: q ? q : undefined` so
     subsequent pages stay filtered server-side. Backend resolver:
     cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifacts.go (search applied server-side).
   - The previous client-side `filtered` derivation has been replaced with
     `$derived(allEdges)` (+page.svelte:53-55) with a comment stating the rationale.

2. Debounced (200ms typical).
   - +page.svelte:64,80-89 — clearTimeout/setTimeout(..., 200) on each keystroke
     before navigateWith fires. Matches the AC's "200ms typical" target.

3. Stale responses dropped on race.
   - searchFetcher.ts:11-54 — createRequestSequence() issues monotonic tokens;
     runLatest() returns `{ stale: true }` if a newer call superseded the in-flight
     one; invalidate() drops all in-flight tokens (used when search query changes).
   - +page.svelte:65,80-89,168-192 — fetchSeq is invalidated on each search input,
     and loadMore() runs through runLatest(fetchSeq, ...). Stale results are
     short-circuited (`if (outcome.stale) return`) so they cannot be appended on
     top of a newer page-1.

4. Tests cover correctness past page 1.
   - searchFetcher.test.ts (8 tests, all passing — verified locally with
     `bunx vitest run searchFetcher` in cli/internal/server/frontend):
       * "marks only the latest token as current"
       * "invalidate() drops all in-flight tokens"
       * "returns false for a never-issued token"
       * "returns the value when no newer call superseded it"
       * "drops the older response when a newer call resolves first" — directly
         simulates the artifacts search race (user types "fo" then "foo"; the
         "fo" page-1 response arrives after "foo" page-1 has painted)
       * "drops a response invalidated mid-flight (e.g. search changed)"
       * "treats successive paginated calls (page 1, page 2) as non-stale when
         uncontended" — explicit page-1/page-2 correctness assertion
       * "swallows errors from stale responses but propagates fresh errors"
   - e2e/artifacts.spec.ts mocks /graphql to honor the `search` variable on
     the server side, so the search input is exercised against the server
     contract end-to-end.

at base-rev a9726cd3 (this worktree's base). The prior BLOCK was on an attempt whose
diff only contained execution evidence; this rationale points at the evidence in the
already-landed commits so a re-review can resolve the BLOCK.

  8d1edfa4 fix(web): drop stale artifacts loadMore responses on search race [ddx-18b15451]

    cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.svelte

    cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/searchFetcher.test.ts

Supporting prior commit (added the `search:` arg to the GraphQL query and wired it
into the SSR load() so search is server-side from page 1, not just past page 1):
  13896420 fix(artifacts): mediaType wildcard + server-side search + sort/staleness args [ddx-0ac5f35d]

AC mapping (all satisfied):

1. Frontend search calls server.
   - +page.ts:7-27 — ARTIFACTS_QUERY declares `$search: String` and forwards it to
     `artifacts(search: $search, ...)`.
   - +page.ts:84-117 — load() reads `q` from the URL and passes it as `search` to the
     server on initial render and on every navigation.
   - +page.svelte:67-78,87 — onSearchInput debounces then calls navigateWith({ q }),
     which goto()s the new URL, re-running load() against the server.
   - +page.svelte:168-192 — loadMore() also passes `search: q ? q : undefined` so
     subsequent pages stay filtered server-side. Backend resolver:
     cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifacts.go (search applied server-side).
   - The previous client-side `filtered` derivation has been replaced with
     `$derived(allEdges)` (+page.svelte:53-55) with a comment stating the rationale.

2. Debounced (200ms typical).
   - +page.svelte:64,80-89 — clearTimeout/setTimeout(..., 200) on each keystroke
     before navigateWith fires. Matches the AC's "200ms typical" target.

3. Stale responses dropped on race.
   - searchFetcher.ts:11-54 — createRequestSequence() issues monotonic tokens;
     runLatest() returns `{ stale: true }` if a newer call superseded the in-flight
     one; invalidate() drops all in-flight tokens (used when search query changes).
   - +page.svelte:65,80-89,168-192 — fetchSeq is invalidated on each search input,
     and loadMore() runs through runLatest(fetchSeq, ...). Stale results are
     short-circuited (`if (outcome.stale) return`) so they cannot be appended on
     top of a newer page-1.

4. Tests cover correctness past page 1.
   - searchFetcher.test.ts (8 tests, all passing — verified locally with
     `bunx vitest run searchFetcher` in cli/internal/server/frontend):
       * "marks only the latest token as current"
       * "invalidate() drops all in-flight tokens"
       * "returns false for a never-issued token"
       * "returns the value when no newer call superseded it"
       * "drops the older response when a newer call resolves first" — directly
         simulates the artifacts search race (user types "fo" then "foo"; the
         "fo" page-1 response arrives after "foo" page-1 has painted)
       * "drops a response invalidated mid-flight (e.g. search changed)"
       * "treats successive paginated calls (page 1, page 2) as non-stale when
         uncontended" — explicit page-1/page-2 correctness assertion
       * "swallows errors from stale responses but propagates fresh errors"
   - e2e/artifacts.spec.ts mocks /graphql to honor the `search` variable on
     the server side, so the search input is exercised against the server
     contract end-to-end.

rationale: All three acceptance criteria for ddx-1978065c are already satisfied in the base
revision (d1342a86) by commit b7dd9f9e (in main):

  feat(web): shared FilterChip primitive + virtualization on Runs [ddx-1978065c]

Evidence in the worktree:

  1. AC1 — Runs page imports the shared chip primitive:
     `import FilterChip from '$lib/components/FilterChip.svelte'`
     at cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/runs/+page.svelte:10
     The same FilterChip is also used by the Artifacts page
     (cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.svelte),
     confirming it was extracted as a shared primitive.

  2. AC2 — Virtualization kicks in past threshold:
     `const VIRTUALIZE_THRESHOLD = 1000` and
     `let virtualized = $derived(edges.length >= VIRTUALIZE_THRESHOLD)` at
     runs/+page.svelte:17 / :155, applied per-row via `style={rowStyle}` at
     runs/+page.svelte:373 (content-visibility: auto + contain-intrinsic-size).
     The table exposes `data-virtualized="true|false"` for tests.

  3. AC3 — Live worker phase updates still work through the virtualized list:
     Virtualization uses CSS content-visibility (browser-native row windowing),
     not row unmount. Expanded rows render <RunRowDetail …/> at line 454; that
     component owns the worker subscription/polling and remains mounted in the
     DOM regardless of virtualization. `content-visibility: auto` only skips
     paint/layout for off-screen rows, so reactive updates to row state and to
     RunRowDetail subscriptions continue to flow.

The previous attempt (ddx-1978065c, run 20260503T064620) was BLOCKED by review
because its merged diff carried only execution manifest/result JSON — but the
actual code from that attempt did land via b7dd9f9e and is present in main.
There is no remaining code change to make for this bead.

+{"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go defines ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, SupportedSpecVersion, ApplyDefaults(), and Validate().
2. ExecuteLoopSpec includes every field named in ddx-29058e2a AC #1, including SpecVersion, FromRev, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, and MaxPower.
3. TestExecuteLoopSpec_DurationJSONUsesStringAndAcceptsNumeric verifies PollInterval and RequestTimeout marshal as strings such as "30s" and unmarshal legacy numeric nanoseconds.
4. TestExecuteLoopSpec_ApplyDefaultsAndValidateVersion verifies default SpecVersion/PollInterval behavior and rejects SpecVersion greater than SupportedSpecVersion with an error that exposes the supported capability/version.
5. TestExecuteLoopSpec_DispatchOptionsNotPersisted verifies marshaling ExecuteLoopSpec never includes local or json fields.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/executeloop/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-04T02:20:24.600246009Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-1a9cc01f","depends_on_id":"ddx-29058e2a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-04T02:20:34Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
Execute-loop execution fields are duplicated across cobra extraction, REST/GraphQL request structs, server ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec persistence, and runWorker consumption. Adding a flag can silently drop it on one transport.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 defines ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec as a server-local struct while cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 extracts a wider flag set, cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 has another request struct, and cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-75 has a third. The type boundary is not shared.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small import-safe package under cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ that defines ExecuteLoopSpec with JSON tags and fields ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Once, PollInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, and SpecVersion.
- Add DispatchOptions with only Local and JSON control-plane fields; do not persist it.
- Add Duration JSON support that marshals as strings and unmarshals both strings and numeric nanoseconds.
- Add ApplyDefaults and Validate methods, including SpecVersion support and a code comment documenting the single-version client/server forward-compat stance.
- Do not wire cobra, REST, GraphQL, or runWorker callers in this bead; later children consume this package.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not rename CLI flags or change LabelFilter from string.
- Do not change execute-loop validation policy beyond moving existing checks behind the spec lifecycle.
- Do not modify server or cobra call sites except imports needed by tests.","id":"ddx-1a9cc01f","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","area:server","kind:refactor","prevention","triage:needs-investigation"],"parent":"ddx-29058e2a","priority":1,"status":"open","title":"agent: introduce canonical ExecuteLoopSpec package","updated_at":"2026-05-04T02:20:34.412757448Z"}
